symptom: Azure Disk Encryption (ADE) fails with error code 2147942487 on Windows VM during BitLocker encryption via AzureDiskEncryption extension
rootCause: BitLocker policy conflict: custom FVE registry keys under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\FVE (e.g. EncryptionMethodWithXtsOs set to XTS_AES_128) conflict with Azure required XTS_AES_256 encryption method
solution: Remove extra custom policy keys under Policies\Microsoft\FVE to restore defaults. If previous failed ADE extension exists, first disable ADE (az vm encryption disable) and delete extension (az vm extension delete -n AzureDiskEncryption) before retrying.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/======= 4. Windows=======/4.9 [Win] Azure Disk Encryption failed to enable w.md

symptom: Customer accidentally deleted a managed disk and needs recovery (disk in soft-deleted state)
rootCause: Managed disk enters soft-deleted state when: created >10 days ago (internal), deleted <96 hours ago, not Ultra Disk. Only DiskRP PG can perform recovery since March 2022.
solution: 1) Check DiskRPResourceLifecycleEvent in Kusto (disksmc cluster) for SoftDelete event. 2) Submit ICM to AzureRT/Disk Service (Sev2: tmpl=U134C9, Sev3: tmpl=i1yp18) with sub ID, source/target RG, disk name. Ensure target RG exists and is non-empty.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======6. Storage=======/6.0 Support matrix for Azure Storage resource reco/6.0.3 _ MCVKB _ How to recover soft delete managed.md

symptom: Customer needs to recover a hard-deleted managed disk
rootCause: Disk is past 96-hour soft-delete window and in HardDelete state. Recovery is best-effort, not guaranteed. If >7 days past hard deletion, non-recoverable.
solution: 1) Check DiskRPResourceLifecycleEvent in Kusto. 2) Run Geneva Action Get Blob Recovery Info (Xstore > Resource Recovery) with blob URI and HardDelete timestamp. 3) If IsRecoverable=true, submit ICM to EEE Storage (Sev3/4: tmpl=a123U3, Sev1/2: tmpl=61A1D6). Deliver disclaimer to customer about non-guaranteed recovery.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======6. Storage=======/6.0 Support matrix for Azure Storage resource reco/6.0.6 _ MCVKB _ hard delete managed disk recovery.md

symptom: VM experiences IaaS Disk Failure (Event 17) causing unexpected restart
rootCause: Storage service availability drop - VM host disk driver cannot write data to storage for 2 minutes, triggering VM restart. TriageCategory indicates root cause: XStore, NetworkingSuspicious, NotXStore.
solution: 1) Query VMA in vmainsight Kusto for TriageCategory. 2) Query XHealth_DiskFailureXStoreTriage in xlivesite Kusto. 3) Run XPortal Event17IncidentData diagnostic. 4) For non-XStore: file ICM to correct PG. For NetworkingSuspicious: also run NetVMA. 5) Deliver general E17 RCA if detailed RCA not requested.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======6. Storage=======/6.16 _ MCVKB _ Xstore _ IaaS Disk Failures TSG.md

symptom: Standard storage billing higher than expected - customer charged for full disk size even after deleting data
rootCause: Azure thin-provisioning: deleted data marks blocks as free in file system but does not notify backend to reclaim space. Without TRIM/UNMAP, page blob retains allocated space.
solution: Linux: use discard mount option or run fstrim manually/crontab. For md RAID on RHEL/CentOS 7.x, set raid0.devices_discard_performance=Y. Windows: verify NTFS DisableDeleteNotify=0 (fsutil behavior query DisableDeleteNotify). Trim enabled by default in Server 2012+.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======6. Storage=======/6.22 _ Trim disks for Windows and Linux to release.md

symptom: TRIM/fstrim does not release space on Windows with dynamic disks; defrag RETRIM fails
rootCause: Defrag RETRIM checks if storage supports TRIM query before performing, but dynamic disks do not support this query. Also slab size differs from cluster size preventing efficient reclamation.
solution: Workaround: create large file to fill volume (fsutil file createnew D:\bigfile.bin <size>; fsutil file seteof D:\bigfile.bin <size>) then delete. Fundamental solution: migrate to Storage Spaces instead of dynamic disks.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======6. Storage=======/6.22 _ Trim disks for Windows and Linux to release.md

symptom: Data loss on Azure VM temporary disk after VM upgrade, migration, or maintenance
rootCause: Temporary disk is local/ephemeral storage wiped during VM maintenance operations. Data on temp disk is not persistent.
solution: Linux: set ResourceDisk.Format=n in /etc/waagent.conf, restart VM. Windows: 1) Remove paging file from temp disk (Advanced System Settings > Performance > Virtual Memory). 2) Restart. 3) Set temp disk offline in Disk Management.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======6. Storage=======/6.3 [Temporary Disk] How to disable temporary disk.md

symptom: Customer needs to recover deleted blob or unmanaged disk data
rootCause: Data deleted at blob level. Recovery window: <6 days for Standard Storage, <3 days for Premium. Best-effort recovery.
solution: 1) Use ASC Blob/Container Deletion Lookup or Jarvis logs for details. 2) Run XPortal VirtualEEEIncidentData to check recoverability. 3) Submit ICM to EEE Storage (Sev3/4: tmpl=a123U3, Sev1/2: tmpl=61A1D6). Route to XStore/GC or XStore/Location Service based on DNS resolution.
sourceRef: MCVKB/VM+SCIM/=======6. Storage=======/6.0 Support matrix for Azure Storage resource reco/6.0.4 _ MCVKB _ Blob recovery (blob, unmanaged dis.md

symptom: Azure VM data disk performance drops dramatically (P30 to 0.11M/s) when temporary disk under heavy IO
rootCause: VM-level cached IO throttling: temp disk shares cached throughput limit with cached data disks. Heavy temp disk usage exhausts VM cached limit causing dependent blocking.
solution: 1) Disable disk cache on mission-critical disks when temp disk used. 2) Ensure total cached throughput < VM cached max. 3) Disable temp disk if not needed. 4) Separate sync IO (SQL log) from async IO disks. 5) Consider RAMDISK for high-perf temp storage.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======VM&SCIM======/_6.1 [Storage][Perf][Azure VM Storage Performance.md

symptom: Azure Premium disk sync IO only achieves ~500 IOPS despite P30 supporting 5000 IOPS
rootCause: Sync IO limited by disk latency (~2ms/IO), not IOPS limit. Single outstanding sync IO = 1000ms/2ms = 500 IOPS. Adding disks does not improve single-thread sync IO.
solution: 1) Increase outstanding IOs/queue depth (10 needed to fully utilize P30). 2) Use async IO where possible. 3) For SQL: tune write IO size/pattern. 4) Ensure no VM-level throttling. 5) Size VM so total throughput stays within limits.
sourceRef: MCVKB/wiki_migration/======VM&SCIM======/_6.1 [Storage][Perf][Azure VM Storage Performance.md

symptom: AKS pod with Azure Disk PV fails to start after migrating to a node in a different availability zone
rootCause: LRS (Locally Redundant Storage) disk only replicates within a single AZ. When AKS scheduler moves pod cross-AZ, the new node cannot access the LRS disk in the original zone.
solution: 1) Use ZRS (Zone-Redundant Storage) disk instead of LRS. 2) If ZRS not feasible (cost/legacy), add nodeAffinity with topology.disk.csi.azure.com/zone to pin pod to the disk's AZ. 3) Check AZ config: az aks show --query 'agentPoolProfiles[].availabilityZones'. Trade-off: node affinity breaks scheduling balance.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Case Study###/[AKS] LRS disk storage.md

symptom: Blobfuse volume mounted in Prometheus pod shows incorrect size matching temp disk instead of storage account capacity, volume fills up and pod terminates
rootCause: Blobfuse volume size equals the node temp disk cache size, not the requested PVC capacity or storage account size. Files with open handles are cached on temp disk consuming actual space; closed files show 0 bytes.
solution: Do not use blobfuse with write-heavy workloads like Prometheus. Use Azure Disk or Azure Files instead. If blobfuse is required, increase cache-size-mb mount option and use nodes with larger temp disks.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/AKS/###Troubleshooting--MUST READ!!###/##Storage in AKS/[Blobfuse] unexpected volume size causing Promethe.md

symptom: Premium SSD disk I/O latency spikes to 1800ms (await), IOPS appears within disk limits but throughput hits VM-level cap
rootCause: VM-level uncached disk throughput throttling. Application writes large I/O blocks (~500KB) at 1300-1400 IOPS, reaching VM max throughput (e.g. DS5_v2 = 768MBps) even though IOPS is within limits. Azure checks throughput every 50ms and queues excess I/O.
solution: 1) Resize VM to support higher throughput (e.g. DS15_v2 for ~1000MBps). 2) Use disk striping (e.g. 2x P60) to distribute I/O. 3) Spread application writes across multiple disks. 4) Monitor VM-level throughput metrics, not just disk-level IOPS.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Disk][Performance]Disk I_O high latency.md

symptom: Data Box Disk: subfolders disappear after Robocopy, dir shows empty but direct path reveals data
rootCause: Source folder contains system-protected data; after Robocopy to Data Box Disk, folders are marked hidden
solution: Protected files do not affect data copy. Run validate tool to confirm files are listed. Data is present but hidden in explorer.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting - Data Prepare.md

symptom: Data Box Disk cannot be opened after unlock - filesystem errors or RAW partition
rootCause: Filesystem corruption or physical disk damage on the Data Box Disk
solution: Disk is unusable. Collect diagnostic data, submit to PG, arrange re-shipment of replacement disk.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting - Data Prepare.md

symptom: Data Box Disk validation fails: Command failed - invalid containers found (ErrorXml)
rootCause: File or folder names do not comply with Azure naming conventions
solution: Check ErrorXml log. Rename non-compliant files to meet Azure naming rules or exclude them. Re-run validation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting - Data Validation.md

symptom: Data Box Disk validation error: Found user files in drive root directory
rootCause: Data files placed directly in disk root instead of required subfolders (BlockBlob/PageBlob/AzureFile/ManagedDisk)
solution: Move files into appropriate subfolder by target storage type. Delete stray root files. Re-run validation.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting - Data Validation.md

symptom: Data Box Disk validation error: Option 2 already executed, reset required
rootCause: Manifest files from previous validation exist. Data modified after initial validation but old manifests remain.
solution: Execute Option 3 (reset) to clear manifests, then re-run Option 1 or 2. Always reset before re-validating after data changes.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting - Data Validation.md

symptom: Data Box Disk unlock tool fails on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7.x
rootCause: Known compatibility issue with RHEL 7.x and Data Box Disk unlock toolset
solution: Refer to internal KB (a2861f24). Verify OS requirements, PowerShell version, prerequisites. Try alternative supported OS.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting disk unlock issues.md

symptom: Data Box Disk import fails or data routes to wrong target due to incorrect folder structure
rootCause: Folder-based routing: BlockBlob/PageBlob/AzureFile/ManagedDisk subfolders map to containers/shares. Root files not allowed.
solution: Organize per target: subfolder = container/share. AzureFile max 5TB/share. ManagedDisk: fixed VHDs only, no duplicates. See data-box-disk-limits doc.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting Data Box Disk/Upload Logic and Limitation.md

symptom: Data Box Disk managed disk import fails: dynamic VHD, VHDX, or duplicate VHD name
rootCause: Only fixed VHDs supported. Dynamic/differencing VHDs and VHDX not supported. Names must be unique across import and target RG.
solution: Convert dynamic to fixed VHD (Hyper-V Edit Disk). Convert VHDX to VHD. Ensure unique names. Place in ManagedDisk/{sku} subfolder.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting Data Box Disk/Upload Logic and Limitation.md

symptom: Data Box POD order creation fails: Data Box model not available in selected region
rootCause: Azure Data Box service does not support all countries/regions. Data ingestion/egress only within same country/region (exception: EU cross-border). Model availability varies by region.
solution: Check region availability at Azure Products by Region page. If region supported but still failing, escalate via IcM to Data Box SaaS team.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Managing Data Box POD/Assisting customers with Data Box POD order status.md

symptom: Data Box POD order creation fails: could not validate address
rootCause: Data Box service uses UPS Address Validator for address validation. Incorrectly formatted address or UPS validator rejects the address.
solution: 1) Verify address format using UPS Address Validator (ups.com/address_validator). 2) If address is valid but order still fails, raise IcM to Data Box SaaS team.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Managing Data Box POD/Assisting customers with Data Box POD order status.md

symptom: Data Box POD order creation fails: could not create order due to missing prerequisites
rootCause: Missing required prerequisites: Azure storage account with access credentials, supported subscription type (MCA/EA/CSP/Sponsorship/MPN), and Owner/Contributor role on the subscription.
solution: Verify: 1) Storage account exists with valid access key. 2) Subscription type is MCA, EA, CSP (not India), Sponsorship, or MPN. 3) User has Owner or Contributor RBAC role. CSP not supported for India.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Managing Data Box POD/Assisting customers with Data Box POD order status.md

symptom: Cannot create additional Data Box POD orders: limit reached
rootCause: Data Box service limits each Azure subscription to 5 active Data Box orders at a time.
solution: Wait for existing orders to complete/cancel, or use a different subscription. Limit is 5 active orders per subscription.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Managing Data Box POD/Assisting customers with Data Box POD order status.md

symptom: Data Box POD managed disk import fails: VHD file not 512-byte aligned, uploaded as page blob instead of managed disk
rootCause: VHD files must be 512-byte aligned for managed disk import. Non-aligned files are rejected and logged as errors in staging storage account.
solution: Ensure VHD is 512-byte aligned before copying. Remove non-aligned file and re-copy a properly aligned VHD. Check copy logs from Connect and Copy page for specific error details.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Managing Data Box POD/Importing managed disks using Data Box POD.md

symptom: Data Box POD managed disk import: VHD copied outside precreated folders uploaded as page blobs instead of managed disks
rootCause: Data Box creates specific share folders (ResourceGroup_MDisk/PremiumSSD|StandardHDD|StandardSSD). VHDs placed outside these precreated folders are treated as regular page blobs, not managed disks.
solution: Always copy VHDs into the precreated tier folders (Premium SSD, Standard HDD, Standard SSD) under the correct resource group share. Do not create custom folders or place VHDs at share root.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Managing Data Box POD/Importing managed disks using Data Box POD.md

symptom: Data Box POD Prepare to Ship fails with 'Scan completed with errors'
rootCause: Customer data does not conform to Azure storage requirements: file/blob naming conventions violated, files/blobs exceed Azure size limits, or container/share names invalid.
solution: 1) Unlock the device. 2) Go to Connect and Copy page, download the issues list. 3) Resolve each error (rename files, fix container names, reduce oversized files). 4) Run Prepare to Ship again. Do NOT skip Prepare to Ship - skipping causes failures during datacenter upload.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Managing Data Box POD/Running Prepare to Ship and returning the Data Box.md

symptom: Data Box POD import order shows 'Completed with errors' - some data not copied to Azure
rootCause: Customer ignored errors/warnings shown during Prepare to Ship, or skipped Prepare to Ship entirely. Data that doesn't conform to Azure storage naming/size rules fails to upload at datacenter.
solution: 1) Review copy logs (verbose + error logs) in a separate container of the storage account. 2) Customer not charged if failure is Microsoft's fault (hardware/software). 3) For data that failed, re-upload via other means (AzCopy, portal, etc.) after fixing naming/format issues.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Managing Data Box POD/Verifying data after import.md

symptom: Data Box POD data copy to Azure has slowed or stalled completely at datacenter
rootCause: Datacenter-side hardware or software issue affecting data copy process.
solution: 1) Check Lens or ASC (Azure Support Center) for relevant auto-generated IcMs. 2) If no IcMs exist for the Data Box job, raise an IcM with XTDataPathTeam for assistance.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Managing Data Box POD/Verifying data after import.md

symptom: Data Box POD device software version mismatch detected in support package (expected version differs from actual)
rootCause: Device firmware update failed or was interrupted, leaving device on an incorrect software version.
solution: Check Get-HcsApplianceInfo.txt in support package at <Device_Logs>/SupportPackage/<Device_Name>/<Device_Serial>/cmdlets/PodCmdLets. Compare expected vs actual version. If mismatch, escalate for device update.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Generating and analyzing support packages.md

symptom: Data Box POD operation fails due to time zone discrepancy on device
rootCause: Device time zone configuration doesn't match expected time zone, causing time-sensitive operations to fail.
solution: 1) Check Get-HcsTimeSettings.txt in support package. 2) Update time zone via local WebUI. 3) Retry failed operation. 4) If still fails, re-check time settings for persistent discrepancy.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Generating and analyzing support packages.md

symptom: Data Box POD network port shows status other than 'Up' in support package diagnostics
rootCause: Network connectivity issue on Data Box device - cable problem, switch issue, or port configuration error.
solution: Check Get-HcsNetInterface.txt in support package. Ports not connected may show blank status (expected). Any connected port showing non-'Up' status requires network troubleshooting (check cable, switch, port config).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Generating and analyzing support packages.md

symptom: Data Box POD cluster resource in 'Failed' state found in support package
rootCause: Cluster resource failure on Data Box device. Note: some resources are designed to be 'Offline' and can be ignored.
solution: Check Get-ClusterResource.txt in support package. Ignore resources in 'Offline' state (by design). Any resource in 'Failed' state requires further investigation - escalate to Data Box engineering.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Generating and analyzing support packages.md

symptom: Data Box POD device not powering on, or amber/red LEDs visible on the unit
rootCause: Hardware failure: power outlet not working, damaged power cable, defective power supply, or residual charge preventing boot.
solution: 1) Try different power outlet. 2) Check/replace power cable. 3) Test each PSU individually. 4) Disconnect all cables for 15+ mins to drain power, then reconnect. 5) If still not powering on, check WebUI for alerts if accessible, collect support package and run diagnostics. 6) Engage TA or raise ICM for device replacement.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD hardware issues.md

symptom: Data Box POD Prepare to Ship running slowly, stuck, or not progressing
rootCause: Prepare to Ship performs data validation, checksum computation, and multiple stages. Large data volume or specific data patterns (e.g., SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND) can cause it to hang or run extremely slowly.
solution: 1) Check WebUI for warnings/errors at each stage. 2) Unlock device -> Connect and Copy -> download issue list XML for exact errors. 3) For SE_METADATA_NOT_FOUND: escalate to TA. 4) For 5TB+ Azure File Share uploads: known limitation. 5) All stages must be green before shipping label is generated.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Prepare to Ship performance issues.md

symptom: Data Box POD data copy job not progressing or stalling during customer-side data transfer
rootCause: Copy tool misconfiguration, parallel jobs conflicting, another application accessing same data, insufficient free space on Data Box, or unsupported file types.
solution: 1) Identify copy tool (Robocopy, AzCopy, etc.) and exact command syntax. 2) Check if parallel jobs running. 3) Verify no other app accessing the data. 4) Check free space via WebUI. 5) Download issue list from Connect and Copy page. 6) Collect support package and engage TA or raise ICM.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Troubleshooting copy tool errors and issues.md

symptom: Unable to access SMB or NFS shares on Data Box POD device
rootCause: Network connectivity issue: DNS resolution failure, incorrect network config, IP conflict, or client network interface problem.
solution: 1) Ping share name from cmd. 2) If name fails, try Data Box IP directly. 3) Try from different client on same network. 4) Restart Data Box and retry. 5) Check Event Viewer (Application, System, SMBClient). 6) Collect support package and engage TA or raise ICM.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Troubleshooting issues with accessing SMB or NFS s.md

symptom: Slow NFS transfer rate between Linux hosts and Data Box POD
rootCause: NFS performance affected by mount options, network configuration, or client-side tuning. Default NFS settings may not be optimal for large data transfers.
solution: Check NFS mount options, network throughput, consider adding additional NFS client IPs for parallel transfers. Refer to content idea: Slow NFS Transfer Rate Between Linux Hosts and Azure Data Box Pod.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Troubleshooting issues with accessing SMB or NFS s.md

symptom: Cannot connect to Data Box POD via REST API - connection failures or authentication errors
rootCause: Incorrect REST connection parameters: wrong storage account name, access key, blob service endpoint, or HTTP vs HTTPS mismatch.
solution: 1) Verify REST connection requirements per docs. 2) Confirm HTTP vs HTTPS. 3) Validate from WebUI Connect and Copy: storage account name, access key, blob service endpoint. 4) Check storage account name and type. 5) Collect support package and engage TA or raise ICM.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Troubleshooting issues with connecting to Data Box.md

symptom: Data Box POD export order: need to verify data integrity and troubleshoot export failures
rootCause: Data export may fail silently or partially. Copy/verbose logs must be checked to confirm all files exported correctly from Azure to Data Box.
solution: 1) Check copy logs at container copylog. 2) Review verbose log for file list with sizes and checksums. 3) Compare exported data against source. 4) Check audit logs at storage-account/azuredatabox-chainofcustodylogs. 5) Enable copy log generation when placing export order.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Troubleshooting issues with exporting data from Az.md

symptom: Unable to access Data Box POD local web UI (WebUI) - page not loading or connection refused
rootCause: Hardware issue (device not powered, cabling problem, LED fault) or network issue (incorrect IP, HTTPS not used, duplicate IP on network, DNS resolution failure).
solution: Hardware: 1) Verify cabling. 2) Check System Fault LED. 3) Confirm power-on. Network: 1) Ping MGMT IP 192.168.100.10 from client at 192.168.100.5. 2) Use HTTPS with IP (not hostname). 3) Check duplicate IPs. 4) Try other port IPs. 5) Cold reboot. 6) Try PowerShell CLI. 7) Engage TA or raise ICM.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Troubleshooting Data Box POD/Troubleshooting web UI access issues and errors.md

symptom: Data Box cross-region scenario confusion in Mooncake: customer wants to export data from China East storage account but no Data Box hardware in China East
rootCause: Data Box POD hardware only exists in specific DC regions (SHA20/ZQZ22 for POD; NTG20/BJS20/ZQZ22 for Disk in Mooncake). Cross-region within same commerce boundary IS supported - device in one region can read/write storage account in another region.
solution: 1) Confirm Data Box can handle cross-region within commerce boundary (e.g. China East SA → device in China East 2). 2) For Azure-to-Azure migration, recommend AzCopy instead of Data Box. 3) Export orders: egress fees apply per standard bandwidth pricing. 4) Cross-cloud transfers (gov-to-public) NOT supported.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Understanding Data Box POD/FAQ.md

symptom: Customer confused about Data Box data transfer fees: import vs export cross-region scenarios
rootCause: Import: cross-region backbone transfer is FREE (covered by Data Box service). Export: standard egress fees apply per Azure bandwidth pricing. Cross-cloud (e.g. gov-to-public) not supported for either direction.
solution: Clarify fee model: 1) Import orders = no cross-region transfer cost within commerce boundary. 2) Export orders = egress charged per https://azure.microsoft.com/pricing/details/bandwidth/. 3) For Azure-to-Azure data movement, always recommend AzCopy (no Data Box needed). 4) Stationary and portable hardware are interchangeable within commerce boundary.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Understanding Data Box POD/FAQ.md

symptom: Azure Premium SSD Shared Disk experiencing throttling or performance issues requiring PG escalation
rootCause: Shared disk backend storage issues require Xstore team investigation. Performance throttling may occur at storage level beyond VM-level IOPS/throughput limits.
solution: ICM escalation path: Xstore/Triage (template: k26O2f). Include blob URL of impacted disk(s). Alternative path: Support - EEE Storage. Reference sample throttling ICM: 201596805.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Shared Disks/ICM.md

symptom: Customer needs to enable simultaneous multi-write access to Azure Premium Shared Disk from multiple VMs
rootCause: Azure Shared Disks support multi-writer via Windows Server Failover Clustering (WSFC) with Cluster Shared Volumes (CSV). Direct multi-writer without clustering coordination is not supported for data consistency.
solution: 1) Create Premium SSD shared disk (maxShares > 1). 2) Attach disk to all Failover Cluster node VMs. 3) In Failover Cluster Manager, add disk to Cluster Shared Volume (CSV). 4) Both nodes can then read/write simultaneously to the CSV mount point. Verified: real-time size changes reflect on all nodes.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Shared Disks/Multi-Writers.md

symptom: Data Box export order fails or data missing: archive tier blobs not exported
rootCause: Archive blobs are NOT supported for Data Box export. Only hot and cool tiers are supported. File history and blob snapshots are also not exported. Maximum 80 TB per export order.
solution: 1) Rehydrate archive blobs to hot/cool tier before creating export order (see Azure docs: archive-rehydrate-overview). 2) Verify all target blobs are in supported tiers. 3) Note: append blobs and NFS 3.0 protocol not supported. 4) Page blobs must be 512-byte aligned.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box POD/Understanding Data Box POD/Requirements and limits.md

symptom: Data Box Disk in Mooncake: customer does not receive notification emails because default mailbox is invalid
rootCause: The default mailbox configured in Mooncake portal cannot receive external emails.
solution: Customer needs to add a valid external mailbox in Data Box order notification settings. Use ASC to check shipment status.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test.md

symptom: Data Box Disk: after data copy completes (success or error), disk immediately enters erasure stage; customer cannot access for troubleshooting
rootCause: By design, disk enters erasure stage regardless of copy result after data copy finishes at datacenter.
solution: Contact Ops team or Azure Support immediately before erasure begins if disk needed for troubleshooting.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test.md

symptom: Data Box Disk MS-Managed shipping: department/company line from address not printed on shipping label
rootCause: Department/Co line field not included in generated shipping label during order creation.
solution: Inform customer department info will not appear on label. Ensure main address line sufficient. DC may call to verify for first-time orders.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test.md

symptom: Data Box Disk: after PC restart, disk requires re-unlock via DataBoxDiskUnlock tool; cannot enter passkey in Windows BitLocker prompt
rootCause: Data Box Disk uses BitLocker encryption but passkey cannot be entered through standard Windows BitLocker dialog.
solution: Run DataBoxDiskUnlock.exe again with passkey from Azure portal after restart. Do not use Windows BitLocker prompt.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/#### Data Box Disk Import with MS-Managed Shipment/4. Prepare Data.md

symptom: Data Box Disk: files copied directly to AzureFile folder root fail and are uploaded as block blobs instead
rootCause: AzureFile subfolders become file shares. Files at root have no target share and default to block blob upload.
solution: Create subfolders under AzureFile for each target file share. Copy data into subfolders, not root.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/#### Data Box Disk Import with MS-Managed Shipment/4. Prepare Data.md

symptom: Data Box Disk checksum validation (Option 2) takes extremely long time for large datasets with many small (KB) files
rootCause: Validation tool processes each file individually; per-file overhead significant for many small files.
solution: Warn customer about long validation time. Option 1 (skip checksum) available but must independently verify in Azure. Reset tool (Option 3) between runs.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/#### Data Box Disk Import with MS-Managed Shipment/4. Prepare Data.md

symptom: MARS offline backup with Data Box Disk: modifying backup items after initial config causes MMC console crash
rootCause: MARS agent MMC snap-in encounters unhandled exception when modifying backup items for existing offline backup schedule.
solution: Avoid modifying backup items after initial config. Delete existing schedule and create new one if needed. Empty Data Box Disk folders before retrying.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/#### Data Box Disk Import with MS-Managed Shipment/5. test MARS offline backup.md

symptom: MARS offline backup with Data Box Disk: backup fails because folder name on disk does not follow fixed format
rootCause: MARS expects specific fixed folder naming on Data Box Disk. Modified/incorrect folder names cause backup failure.
solution: Do not rename Data Box Disk folder structure. Follow exact naming convention from Azure Backup docs. Delete existing files before retrying with correct names.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/#### Data Box Disk Import with MS-Managed Shipment/5. test MARS offline backup.md

symptom: Azure Shared Disks: Kusto query for OsXIOXdiskCounterTable on rdosmc cluster to troubleshoot shared disk IO issues in Mooncake
rootCause: Shared disk investigation requires querying OsXIOXdiskCounterTable on rdosmc.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/rdos with BlobPath filter.
solution: Query: OsXIOXdiskCounterTable | where PreciseTimeStamp > ago(5d) | where BlobPath has '<blob-path>' | take 10
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Azure Shared Disks/Troubleshooting.md

symptom: Data Box Disk validation error: InvalidShareContainerFormat when container/share/fileshare names contain uppercase letters (e.g. BC2SOFTWARE2, pc1-RHEL, Pc2-suse, FS2-music)
rootCause: Azure storage naming conventions require all lowercase for container, page blob container, and file share names. Data Box Disk validation rejects names with uppercase characters.
solution: Rename all subfolders under BlockBlob/PageBlob/AzureFile to use only lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens. Re-run validation after correction.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/#### Data Box Disk Import with Self-Managed Shipme/5. prepare data.md

symptom: Data Box Disk requires re-unlock after host PC reboot or USB reconnection
rootCause: By design, Data Box Disk lock re-engages after the disk is disconnected or the host reboots. The unlock state is not persistent across reconnections.
solution: After reboot or USB reconnection, re-run the Data Box Disk unlock tool with the passkey before resuming data copy operations.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/#### Data Box Disk Import with Self-Managed Shipme/5. prepare data.md

symptom: Data Box Disk USB drive disappears or gets locked during Robocopy large file copy (both local and MS disk ejected simultaneously, then re-detected)
rootCause: Host PC USB interface instability under sustained I/O load (especially with Robocopy /MT:64 or /MT:8). All removable drives on the same USB controller may be ejected.
solution: Reduce Robocopy thread count (e.g. /MT:4), use File Explorer for copying, or use a different USB 3.0 port/hub. After ejection, reboot host and re-unlock the Data Box Disk.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/#### Data Box Disk Import with Self-Managed Shipme/5. prepare data.md

symptom: Data Box Disk: files copied under PageBlob folder that do not meet page blob format (e.g., non-VHD files, ISO images) are moved to a container named databox-invalid-pb-<jobname> instead of the target container
rootCause: Data Box Disk validates PageBlob files during data copy at DC. Files that are not valid page blobs (must be 512-byte aligned VHD) are automatically redirected to an invalid container. Files placed directly at PageBlob root are also treated as block blobs.
solution: Ensure only valid fixed VHD files are placed under PageBlob subfolders. Non-VHD files should go under BlockBlob. Check the databox-invalid-pb-<jobname> container for any misplaced files and re-upload if needed.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/#### Data Box Disk Import with Self-Managed Shipme/9. Log analysis.md

symptom: Data Box Disk: when multiple disks in same order contain subfolders with same name (e.g., bc-dup on Disk1 and Disk2), customer expects separate containers but data is merged into a single container
rootCause: Data Box Disk import creates containers/shares by folder name. When same folder name exists across multiple disks, data from all disks is merged into the same Azure container or file share. This is by design.
solution: Inform customer that duplicate folder names across disks will result in data merging into one container. If separate containers are needed, use unique folder names on each disk. Data is not lost but combined.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/#### Data Box Disk Import with Self-Managed Shipme/9. Log analysis.md

symptom: Data Box Disk Mooncake: customer complains disk preparation takes too long after order is placed, no estimated SLA shown in portal or order email
rootCause: Disk preparation in Mooncake can take up to 5 business days (FAQ). CE3 region observed 3 days, BJB took 1 day. SLA only documented in FAQ, not in order workflow or notifications.
solution: Set customer expectation: disk preparation may take up to 5 business days per FAQ. If exceeding, engage Data Box operations. Ref: docs.azure.cn/en-us/databox/data-box-disk-faq
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/Doc verification.md

symptom: Data Box Disk self-managed shipment Mooncake: after disks are ready, customer still needs to wait 1-2 business days before pickup due to DC security record application
rootCause: In self-managed shipment mode, DC requires security record for anyone entering the gate. This takes 1-2 working days. In practice, carriers in China do pickup/dropoff at gate where DC operator takes package, so security record may not be strictly necessary.
solution: Inform customer about 1-2 business day wait after disks ready for security record. Once operation team provides Authorization code in portal, customer can contact DC operator. DC only operates on business days.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/Doc verification.md

symptom: Data Box Disk Mooncake return: phone number 400.889.6066 ext. 3693 in documentation for disk return contact is unreachable
rootCause: Phone number in return documentation may be outdated or extension incorrect. Email contact confirmed to work.
solution: Use email instead of phone to contact return team. If customer needs phone support, escalate to Data Box operations for updated contact. Verify China North DC contact in notification email.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/Doc verification.md

symptom: Data Box Disk: data copy at DC does not start for several days after disk status changes to Received, no SLA published for time between received and data copy start
rootCause: No published SLA for how long after disk received at DC data copy begins. Testing showed within 2 hours, but customer cases show delays of several days. Most common cause is shipping label mismatch (customer used different tracking ID), causing information mismatch and action delay.
solution: 1) Confirm customer used original shipping label. 2) If label changed, engage Data Box operations to manually match. 3) If waiting >2 business days with correct label, escalate to operations. No official SLA exists.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Data Box Disk process test/Doc verification.md

symptom: Data Box Disk: data copied into user-created folder (not one of the precreated folders on the disk) fails to upload to Azure
rootCause: Data Box Disk creates precreated folder structures (BlockBlob, PageBlob, AzureFile with subfolders) based on order config. Folders manually created by customer are not recognized by import process and data in them will not be uploaded.
solution: Instruct customer to only copy data into precreated folders on the unlocked disk. Do not create new top-level or sub-folders. If precreated folder does not exist, order configuration may need updating.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Managing Data Box Disk/Copy data for import to Azure Files, blobs, or pag.md

symptom: Data Box Disk managed disk import: customer uploads dynamic VHD, differencing VHD, or VHDX files but they are not imported as managed disks
rootCause: Data Box Disk import to managed disks only supports fixed VHDs. Dynamic VHDs, differencing VHDs, and VHDX files are not supported for managed disk creation.
solution: Convert VHDs to fixed format before copying to Data Box Disk. Use Hyper-V Manager or Convert-VHD PowerShell cmdlet: Convert-VHD -Path source.vhdx -DestinationPath dest.vhd -VHDType Fixed. VHDX must also be converted to VHD format.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Managing Data Box Disk/Import data to managed disks.md

symptom: Data Box Disk validation tool or Split Copy tool reports failures when file paths exceed 256 characters and long paths not enabled on the client
rootCause: Windows default path length limit is 260 characters. If long paths not enabled on client machine and any path+filename exceeds 256 chars, DataBoxDiskValidation.cmd and DataBoxDiskSplitCopy.exe will report errors.
solution: Enable long path support on client: 1) Group Policy: Computer Config > Admin Templates > System > Filesystem > Enable Win32 long paths. 2) Registry: HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem\LongPathsEnabled = 1. 3) Or restructure data to use shorter paths.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Managing Data Box Disk/Import data to managed disks.md

symptom: Data Box Disk managed disk import: Split Copy tool cannot be used for managed disk scenarios, and duplicate managed disk names within same resource group cause import failure
rootCause: Split Copy tool is not supported for managed disk imports. Only one managed disk with a given name can exist in a resource group across all precreated folders and all Data Box Disks in the order.
solution: Do not use Split Copy tool for managed disk scenarios. Ensure each VHD file has unique name within target resource group. If importing from multiple disks/folders, verify no naming conflicts exist across all precreated managed disk folders.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Managing Data Box Disk/Import data to managed disks.md

symptom: Data Box Disk upload log shows FileNotFound error for files that existed during copy but were moved or deleted after running validation (DataBoxDiskValidation.cmd)
rootCause: Customer ran validation (Option 2 checksum), then moved or deleted some source files before the disk was returned. DC-side upload fails because checksum manifest references files that no longer exist on disk.
solution: No Azure-side fix. Customer must reorder a new Data Box Disk and re-copy data without modifying files after validation. Advise customer to always keep source files intact until upload is confirmed complete.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting - Data Copy(Data Upload ).md

symptom: Data Box Disk portal shows 'Copy Completed' status but copy logs (XML) contain errors such as InvalidPageBlobUploadAsBlockBlob or MovedToDefaultAzureShare
rootCause: Customer did not run validation before shipping disk. Files that do not conform to the target blob type or folder structure are moved to fallback containers (databox-invalid-pb-<jobname> or databox-<jobname>) at DC. Portal still shows 'Completed' because the copy process finished, but some files ended up in unexpected locations.
solution: 1. Download and review the copy log XML from portal. 2. Check for Blob Status entries like InvalidPageBlobUploadAsBlockBlob, MovedToDefaultAzureShare. 3. Verify data in the fallback containers. 4. For future orders, always run DataBoxDiskValidation.cmd (Option 2) before shipping.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/Data Box/Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting Data Box Disk/Troubleshooting - Data Copy(Data Upload ).md

symptom: Azure File Sync StorageFileLogs shows unclear operation names (CreateDirectory, CreateFile, RenameFile, PutRange) during initial sync to Azure Files
rootCause: CreateDirectory and CreateFile write metadata only. PutRange writes actual file data. RenameFile creates both folders and files. Initial sync creates directories first (~41w ops/hour), then files (~10w ops/hour with CreateFile/RenameFile/PutRange)
solution: When analyzing StorageFileLogs for AFS: expect CreateDirectory/RenameFile dominance in first 24h (folder creation phase), then CreateFile/RenameFile/PutRange (file phase). No file count growth in first 24h is normal. D8s v3 with P30 SSD: 500w files/500GB took ~3 days. Memory usage ~17GB initially, dropping to 10GB. Decommission guide: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/file-sync/file-sync-server-endpoint-delete
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Azure File Sync/Azure file sync performance/File sync.md

symptom: Robocopy migration to Azure Files (SMB) shows no file creation activity for the first 2+ hours
rootCause: Robocopy processes directory structure before files. For large datasets with millions of folders, the initial CreateDirectory phase via SMB takes hours before any actual file content is copied
solution: This is expected behavior. Monitor StorageFileLogs for SMB CreateDirectory operations to confirm progress. File creation begins only after folder structure is established. For 190w files/385w folders/180GB, the folder phase took ~5 hours. Use StorageFileLogs query: summarize request_count = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h), SmbCommandMinor
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Azure File Sync/Azure file sync performance/Robocopy.md

symptom: Need to determine optimal Robocopy /MT thread count for migrating large number of small files to Azure Files via SMB
rootCause: Azure Files SMB performance varies with thread count. Too few threads underutilize bandwidth; too many overwhelm the storage account or exceed CPU capacity
solution: Microsoft recommends /MT:8-20 for initial copy, up to /MT:64 for subsequent runs if CPU can sustain. Tested with B4ms (4 vCPU): /MT:20 completed 190w files/180GB in 11.5h. Recommended flags: /R:2 /W:1 /B /MIR /IT /COPY:DATSO /DCOPY:DAT /NP /NFL /NDL. Monitor with StorageFileLogs: summarize avg(DurationMs), avg(ServerLatencyMs) by OperationName, SmbCommandMinor. Robocopy outperforms Azcopy for small file scenarios (11.5h vs 21h+ at 30%)
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Azure File Sync/Azure file sync performance/Robocopy (2).md

symptom: Blobfuse2 rm -rf command fails on mounted Azure Blob storage container - recursive deletion not supported
rootCause: Blobfuse2 is a FUSE driver that maps file operations to REST API calls. It does not support recursive syscalls (unlink/rmdir) natively because each deletion requires an HTTP request. Without HNS the flat blob namespace cannot handle recursive operations
solution: 1) Upgrade storage account to enable HNS (Data Lake Storage Gen2). 2) Mount blobfuse2 with --use-adls=true flag. Workaround without HNS: delete files first then use rmdir on empty folders
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[2508140040001237] Blobfuse2 can't run _rm -rf_.md

symptom: Storage account firewall modification fails with StorageAccountOperationInProgress error (409 Conflict) - exclusive lock held by previous operation
rootCause: A previous PATCH operation on the storage account acquired an exclusive lock that was not properly released. SRP logs show conflicting locks from an operation days or weeks earlier
solution: 1) Get ARM operation ID from HttpIncomingRequests (armmcadx). 2) Find conflicting lock ID in SRP logs (Geneva portal). 3) Search the original lock requestId in ARM logs. 4) Engage PG (Storage SRP team) to manually release the orphaned exclusive lock
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Account][Blob]Unable to upload data to Storage ac.md

symptom: Storage account returns 404 ContainerNotFound after stamp migration - requests routed to old stamp due to stale DNS/hosts
rootCause: Customer environment had stale DNS entries (hardcoded IP in hosts file) pointing to old storage stamp. After migration requests go to old IP resulting in 404
solution: 1) Check Xportal/backend logs for stamp migration. 2) Ask customer to ping/nslookup storage FQDN and compare IPs. 3) Check hosts file for hardcoded entries. 4) Update hosts/DNS to current IP
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Account][Blob]Unable to upload data to Storage ac.md

symptom: Storage account firewall blocks portal access with IP not authorized error even though client IP was added to firewall rules
rootCause: Organization has multiple outbound IPs. The IP used for Portal requests differs from the actual outbound IP used for xstore data plane requests due to network routing/NAT
solution: 1) Get storage request ID from error. 2) Use Xportal Log Collector to find actual client IP in backend logs. 3) Ask customer IT to add ALL outbound IPs. Alternative: enable Storage Diagnostic Settings to capture real source IP
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Account][Network] Firewall setting blocked xstore.md

symptom: Storage account returns ServerBusyError (503) / ServerAccountBandwidthThrottlingError during load test - egress not hitting account-level limits
rootCause: Stamp-level bandwidth throttling not account-level. ServerAccountBandwidthThrottlingError = stamp-level; ClientAccountBandwidthThrottlingError = account-level
solution: 1) Check Jarvis Shoebox metrics for ServerBusy. 2) Xportal auto-analysis to confirm error type. 3) Query XAggAccountUsageMetric to verify stamp vs account throttling. 4) Request PG to migrate SA to higher-bandwidth stamp
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Account][Performance] storage account level Perfo.md

symptom: Storage account high E2E latency (MAX up to 24s) on GetBlob operations - random transient spikes causing application timeouts
rootCause: Partition merge operation on storage stamp caused transient TableServer latency spike (TotalPartitionWaitTimeInMs). High latency lasted ~1 minute. P99 remained stable at ~100ms
solution: 1) Check Jarvis Shoebox API Dashboard for E2E vs server latency. 2) Xportal auto-analysis: look for TotalPartitionWaitTimeInMs. 3) For transient spikes: recommend application retry logic. 4) Enable Storage Diagnostic logs for future investigation
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Account][Performance]Storage account latency trou.md

symptom: Storage account blob access returns InternalError from portal - CMK encryption configured but MSI identity is None
rootCause: Storage account has CMK encryption with Key Vault but MSI was set to None via CLI. Without MSI the SA cannot authenticate to Key Vault to retrieve encryption key
solution: 1) Check Xportal metadata: msiIdentityType=None with keyVaultUri configured. 2) Search ARM logs for PATCH that changed --identity-type. 3) Fix: re-assign MSI identity. 4) Customer misconfiguration: CLI allows setting identity to None with CMK enabled
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Account][SRP] Storage account access InternalErro.md

symptom: Premium storage account (BlockBlobStorage) shows Access Tier Hot in portal - access tier not supported for premium
rootCause: Portal submits unnecessary metadata (accessTier: Hot) in PUT/PATCH when modifying unrelated settings. SRP accepts unsupported metadata for premium accounts. Bug in both Portal and SRP
solution: 1) Check Xportal metadata for accessTier. 2) Query ARM logs for the PUT/PATCH. 3) Check ShoeboxEntries for full request body. 4) File IcM against Portal PG and SRP PG. 5) Cosmetic issue only - no functional impact
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Account][SRP] Why premium storage account get acc.md

symptom: Storage REST API (CheckNameAvailability) returns 404 because Microsoft.Storage resource provider not registered on subscription
rootCause: After bug fix deployed Aug 2 2022, CheckNameAvailability validates subscription RP registration. Unregistered RP returns 404 SubscriptionNotFound.
solution: Register Microsoft.Storage RP on the subscription. Normally RP auto-registers on first storage resource creation, but manual registration needed if calling API before any resource exists.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Account][SRP] storage resource provider registrat.md

symptom: Azure File Sync recall fails with error 0x80070032 due to unsupported characters in file path
rootCause: Files contain unsupported Unicode characters. Evaluation Tool and ScanUnsupportedChars may miss some. Error logged under Event ID 9006.
solution: Run FileSyncErrorsReport.ps1 to identify affected files. Verify FilePath in error output. Rename files to remove unsupported characters or acknowledge if renaming not possible.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[AzureFileSync] File Recall Error - unsupported ch.md

symptom: Azure File Sync agent v15.0.0 causes high CPU on server after installation
rootCause: Bug in AFS agent v15.0.0 - tiering status bootstrapper thread consumes excessive CPU. ICM 308343342.
solution: Set registry TieringStatusBootstrapMaxThreadCount=0 under HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Azure\StorageSync, then restart filesyncsvc.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[AzureFileSync] High CPU after installing Azure Fi.md

symptom: Blob upload via Java SDK intermittently fails with AuthenticationFailed (403)
rootCause: Client system clock drift >15min tolerance, or high CPU/network causing signed request to take >15min reaching server, making auth header stale.
solution: 1. Sync client clock with NTP. 2. Check client CPU/network. 3. Update SDK. 4. Enable Storage Diagnostics Settings. 5. Enable app debug logging if SDK issue suspected.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Blob] AuthencationFailed Error when uploading blo.md

symptom: Blob Lifecycle Management policy rule does not execute - blobs under certain prefixes not deleted
rootCause: Missing configuration on Storage clusters processing the account. Platform-side bug on specific clusters.
solution: Check Jarvis: ETWEventOLCMScannerStatesTable, ETWEventOLCMAccountPolicyExecutionStatsTable. Check ASC for policy/operations. If no errors, escalate to xstore PG via ICM.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Blob][Lifecycle Management]_how to troubleshot li.md

symptom: Managed disk (Standard SSD) performance significantly worse in China East vs China East 2 with same VM size
rootCause: VM in CE on RDSSD node (AllDisksInStripe) - cache policy forced to None. VM in CE2 on AllDisksAbc node - read/write cache works. Non-premium VM SKUs cannot land on ABC nodes on Gen4.
solution: Use premium-enabled VM SKU (e.g. DS15_v2) to land on AllDisksAbc node. Or change region. Kusto: LogNodeSnapshot.diskConfiguration to verify node type.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[Disk][Performance] 做过一些Azure托管磁盘的技能测试，结果是在中国东部1和东.md

symptom: NFS 4.1 Encryption in Transit (aznfs) mount fails in Mooncake: stunnel rejects cert with 'unable to get local issuer certificate' for DigiCert Basic RSA CN CA G2
rootCause: Mooncake cloud still uses DigiCert Global Root CA (G1), but the aznfs/stunnel config pins DigiCert Global Root G2. During G1→G2 root CA migration (completing by Feb 2026), the cert chain verification fails because stunnel CAfile points to G2.pem which the MC storage endpoint doesn't yet use.
solution: Temp workaround: concatenate both G1 and G2 certs into the stunnel CAfile. Debian: mv G2.pem → backup, cat G1.pem + G2_backup → G2.pem. RHEL: extract G1/G2 from tls-ca-bundle.pem, concatenate. SUSE: same as Debian. After G2 migration completes, restore original G2.pem. If CA missing on VM, download from digicert.com and install via update-ca-certificates (Debian/SUSE) or update-ca-trust extract (RHEL).
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[NFS]NFS4.1 Encryption in Transit(aznfs).md

symptom: AKS CSI NFS 4.1 mount with encryptInTransit=true fails in Mooncake: stunnel reports 'Rejected by CERT at depth=1: CN=DigiCert Basic RSA CN CA G2' but openssl s_client shows cert chain is trusted
rootCause: AKS CSI driver uses stunnel for NFS EiT. Stunnel has its own CA store separate from system trust store, so even though openssl trusts the chain, stunnel rejects it because its CAfile only has G2 but MC uses G1 root. AKS PG (xiazhang@) indicated NFS EiT support in China is not confirmed.
solution: Same cert concatenation workaround as non-AKS scenario. For AKS, can also deploy a privileged pod with stunnel manually configured (K8s pod YAML with stunnel.conf mounting NFS via 127.0.0.1:20049). StorageClass needs: protocol: nfs, encryptInTransit: 'true', specify storageAccount and resourceGroup explicitly. AKS Identity needs Contributor on VNET RG and Storage Account.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[NFS]NFS4.1 Encryption in Transit(aznfs).md

symptom: Azcopy fails with 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority' when copying blobs through proxy server
rootCause: Server running azcopy is missing DigiCert Global Root CA in its certificate store. Browser can tolerate cert errors but azcopy/Go HTTP client enforces strict TLS validation. The cert chain *.blob.core.chinacloudapi.cn → DigiCert Basic RSA CN CA G2 → DigiCert Global Root CA cannot be verified without root CA.
solution: Install the DigiCert Global Root CA on the server. Export from a working machine and import to the affected server. Also: (1) azcopy logs show 'x509: certificate signed by unknown authority' - always check this first; (2) proxy protocol must be http:// not https:// for CONNECT tunnel; (3) HTTPS_PROXY env var is auto-detected on Windows, manual set overrides.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Case Study/[azcopy]The server that azcopy located has no corr.md

symptom: Azcopy sync performance insufficient for large-scale small file migration (millions of files) to Azure File Share; 500GB/5M files takes days at 60%
rootCause: Azcopy sync must index both source and destination before transferring, consuming significant memory and time with millions of files. Increasing concurrency provides diminishing returns for small files due to per-file overhead.
solution: Use Azure File Sync for real-time continuous sync with minimal cutover downtime. Alternative: Robocopy with /MT threads - lab test shows 180GB/2M files/4M folders in 12h. For blob scenarios with 500TB/1B files, use multiple azcopy instances in parallel with custom sync tooling for cutover. Azure Files migration docs recommend Robocopy over azcopy for SMB file share migrations.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Customer Tools/Azcopy/Customer solution.md

symptom: Blobfuse config values not taking effect as expected; environment variable overrides config file settings
rootCause: Blobfuse configuration priority order (highest to lowest): CLI parameter > Environment variable > Config file. If account name/key defined in both env var and config file, the env var value wins silently.
solution: Check all three config sources for conflicting values. Review CLI params first, then env vars (AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT, AZURE_STORAGE_ACCESS_KEY etc.), then config file. CSI driver comes pre-packaged with blobfuse - no manual install needed on AKS.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Feature Readiness/Blobfuse.md

symptom: Storage account connections fail after Azure Storage TLS 1.0/1.1 retirement (Feb 2026 deadline); clients using deprecated TLS versions get rejected
rootCause: Azure Storage retired TLS 1.0 and 1.1 in Feb 2026. Clients still using old TLS versions cannot establish connections. Applies to both Global and Mooncake. No further deadline extensions available.
solution: 1) Migrate all clients to TLS 1.2. 2) SAP coding: Azure\Storage Account Management\Encryption\TLS 1.0 and 1.1 retirement. 3) Root Cause: 'TLS version not supported on the storage account'. 4) TSG: Storage TLS 1.0 and 1.1 Support Ending wiki. 5) Global doc: learn.microsoft.com/azure/storage/common/transport-layer-security-configure-migrate-to-tls2. 6) China doc: docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/storage/common/transport-layer-security-configure-migrate-to-tls2. 7) Check min TLS version: Get-AzStorageAccount or az storage account show.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Feature Readiness/Deprecated Features/Nov 1, 2025, Azure Blob Storage will stop supporti.md

symptom: Customer confused by legacy Blob Storage / GPv1 account migration notification: unclear which account types affected, selective notification delivery, read-only Databricks managed accounts cannot self-migrate
rootCause: Retirement covers BOTH legacy Azure Blob Storage accounts AND GPv1 accounts (deadline Sep-Oct 2026). Notifications only sent to subset of affected subscriptions (not all). Databricks-managed storage accounts have read-only access for customers.
solution: 1) Use Resource Graph query to find affected accounts: Resources | where type == 'microsoft.storage/storageaccounts' | where sku.name in~ ('Standard_LRS','Standard_GRS','Standard_ZRS','Standard_RAGRS','Standard_RAGZRS') | where kind != 'StorageV2'. 2) For Databricks managed accounts (read-only), customer cannot self-migrate - PG will auto-migrate before deadline. 3) Mooncake doc: docs.azure.cn/zh-cn/storage/common/general-purpose-version-1-account-migration-overview. 4) Upgrade guide: docs.azure.cn/en-us/storage/common/storage-account-upgrade.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Feature Readiness/Deprecated Features/Retirement_ Migrate your legacy Azure Blob Storage.md

symptom: Storage billing latency: billing delayed for storage accounts with too many large-sized accounts in a single billing stream
rootCause: Billing jobs process non-uniformly distributed streams; large-sized accounts in a single stream cause processing bottleneck and billing latency.
solution: PG rolling out improved techniques to create more uniformly distributed billing streams. No customer-side action required. If customer reports delayed billing, check ICM 187359813 for status.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Known Issue.md

symptom: AKS pods with Azure File Share (AFS) crash after storage account migration; DNS resolution fails after storage key expiry causing mount failures
rootCause: Linux kernel DNS resolver bug in Ubuntu-azure-5.15.0-1060 and later: after key expiry, kernel cannot reclaim expired key and perform DNS lookup, causing DNS resolution failure for AFS mount. Root cause: kernel commit 39299bdd2546 introduced the regression.
solution: Upgrade AKS node image to AKSUbuntu-2204gen2containerd-202407.29.0+ (kernel 5.15.0-1068-azure includes fix). Check current kernel: kubectl describe node <node-name>. Fix commit: https://git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git/commit/?id=9da27fb65a14c18efd4473e2e82b76b53ba60252
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Known Issue/Storage account migration cause AKS pod with AFS c.md

symptom: Vulnerability scan detects legacy cipher suite (3DES EDE CBC / TLS_RSA_WITH_3DES_EDE_CBC_SHA) on storage account private endpoint NIC even with TLS 1.2 minimum enforced
rootCause: Azure Storage enforces TLS version at HTTP application layer, not TCP/TLS network layer. Port scanning tools (SSL Labs, etc.) probe TCP layer and see legacy cipher suites because Azure Storage is a multi-tenant service maintaining backward compatibility at the network layer.
solution: Expected behavior - safe to ignore scan result. With TLS 1.2 enforced, any request using insecure TLS version gets HTTP 4xx error. Doc: https://docs.microsoft.com/azure/storage/common/transport-layer-security-configure-minimum-version. Advise customer: (1) TLS 1.2 enforcement protects data in transit, (2) scan result reflects network-layer capability not actual policy, (3) Azure Policy + Azure Monitor can enforce/monitor TLS across accounts.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/Known Issue/[Storage][security] vulnerability scan.md

symptom: Geneva Action cannot be triggered in national clouds (Mooncake/Fairfax): operations require escort access or Run with Approval despite working in public cloud
rootCause: National clouds have stricter security policy. Geneva Actions with combined PSA+PSO+PSV claims use the most restrictive one (PSA) to map access policy. PSA maps to ElevatedPrivileged which requires escort in Mooncake and approval in Fairfax. Mixing scope levels (Storage-PSA, Storage-PSO, Storage-PSV) on same operation is incorrect.
solution: Adjust Geneva Action claims: each operation should have ONE role within a scope (not PSA+PSO+PSV combined). Create a read-only base class for basic operations. For urgent needs, use escort access (Get Access button) in Mooncake. Remove unscoped PlatformServiceViewer in favor of scoped Storage-* roles. ICM ref: 426906186.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/MISC/(deprecated) Geneva Action/Issue - Cann't trigger GA in national clouds.md

symptom: Standard disk VM hitting 500 IOPS limit causing high CPU load and kworker IO wait; application performance degrades at regular intervals (e.g. 8:00/20:00 daily)
rootCause: Standard managed disk has 500 IOPS limit per disk. Application (open-falcon Graph) receives too many IO requests due to consistent Hash load imbalance. When IOPS limit reached, IOs are blocked causing kernel kworker IO wait and high CPU load. kworker 99.99% IO does not mean CPU exhaustion, it means process is waiting for IO.
solution: 1) Check VM disk metrics to verify disk IOPS hitting 500 limit. 2) Upgrade disk tier (Standard to Premium) or resize disk for more IOPS. 3) Fix application-level load imbalance (add virtual nodes for consistent hash, recommended 150 virtual nodes per physical server). 4) Separate read/write workloads across multiple disks. 5) Use storage pool to aggregate IOPS.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/MISC/Case triage -4_18_2019/1. Disk Limitation.md

symptom: Managed disk snapshot fails with error: The snapshot rate was exceeded for a disk. Occurs when 3rd party backup (Commvault) and ASR both try to snapshot same disk
rootCause: Managed disk snapshot rate limit is once per minute (applies to both premium and standard managed disks). When Commvault backup and A2A (ASR) both initiate snapshots against the same disk within one minute, the second snapshot request is rejected. Note: docs.microsoft.com states page blob snapshot limit is 10 min, but this applies to unmanaged disks only.
solution: 1) Stagger backup schedules: ensure Commvault and ASR do not snapshot same disk within 1 minute. 2) Query DiskManagerApiQoSEvent table in Jarvis to identify which disk failed. 3) Query DiskManagerContextActivityEvent to see concurrent snapshot operations and their source (ASR vs 3rd party). 4) If using managed disks, the actual limit is 1 snapshot/minute, not 10 minutes as documented for page blobs.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/MISC/Case triage -4_18_2019/2. Snapshot rate limit on Azure page blob.md

symptom: Azure Storage portal access fails in Google Chrome with IPAuthorization or Permission errors after Chrome Local Network Access Check update
rootCause: Chrome Local Network Access Checks block certain requests needed for Azure Storage authentication with Entra User Account. Chrome-specific; other browsers unaffected.
solution: Disable Chrome flag: Local Network Access Check. Update SAP to Azure/Portal/Resource Blade Does Not Load or Refresh. Root cause: Azure Portal/Framework/Core framework issue. Link SR to ICM 721856735.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/MISC/Storage Account Access _ Google Chrome (ONLY)_ New.md

symptom: Cannot identify client IP for storage audit: client behind private link shows IPv6 address in Jarvis logs
rootCause: Private Link or Service Endpoint translates client IP to IPv6. Mapping back requires cross-referencing NRP data.
solution: Step 1: Get client IPv6 from Jarvis. Step 2: ASC Storage Account Tools convert IPv6 to IPv4. Step 3: Query NRP Geneva 293FC026 to find NIC. Step 4: ASC find VM by NIC.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/MISC/TS Flow_ Mapping between IPV6 & IPV4.md

symptom: SRP API throttling: TooManyRequests for storage resource provider operations (Read/List/Write) per subscription per region
rootCause: SRP throttling limits are hard (not raisable), per region per subscription. ARM categorizes by operation type not HTTP method. GET can be List not just Read.
solution: Query RegionalSRP for throttling keywords. Use Geneva 91A6CDBB. Filter by operationName not HTTP method. Reduce API frequency or distribute across regions. Ref: https://aka.ms/srpthrottlinglimits
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/MISC/[API Throttling]_[SRP] How to calculate Read, List.md

symptom: SSE appears to have Disable option in portal but saving fails; customer asks if SSE can be disabled
rootCause: SSE enabled by default for ALL storage accounts (Classic and ARM), cannot be disabled. Disable option is portal UI bug.
solution: SSE cannot be disabled. Check status: Get-AzStorageAccount, check Encryption.Services.blob.Enabled. Contact SSE team: ssediscussions@microsoft.com.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/MISC/[Account]Server Side Encryption.md

symptom: Unable to delete ARM storage account: error AccountPendingMigrationToSrp (Conflict 409)
rootCause: RSRP bug: RDFE-to-SRP migration commit failed, account stuck in Prepare state. Operations blocked in Prepare state.
solution: Try Remove-AzStorageAccount -Force. If fails, file ICM to Xstore triage. Check migration state in Geneva 9BB530D5.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/MISC/[Account]Unable to delete ARM storage account with.md

symptom: Blob download fails when blob name has special Unicode multi-byte chars; old Java SDK encodes differently
rootCause: Some Unicode chars consist of 2 chars but display as 1 glyph. Old Java SDK converts to single char, causing name mismatch.
solution: Upgrade to latest Java SDK.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/MISC/[Blob][SDK]Special letter in the blob name.md

symptom: VM reboots repeatedly with E17 IaaSxStorageOutage; VhdDiskPrt errors XDiskBlobNotFound/XDiskContainerNotFound
rootCause: Managed disk backend blob deleted or container not found at xstore layer. FASTPATH_RESPONSE_TYPE_BUSY during writes.
solution: Run GetRDOSE17Triage on rdosmc.kusto.chinacloudapi.cn/rdos. Check XPortal AutoTriage. If 8170012F/8170012E dominant, escalate to XSTORE/Triage.
sourceRef: Mooncake POD Support Notebook/POD/VMSCIM/4. Services/STORAGE/PG Connection/Xstore Triage Livesite Shadowing/10-31 437170407 High Data Disk Queue Depth on disk.md
Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet
symptom: Write-heavy workload with ReadOnly cache policy causes NFS slot exhaustion, cache recycling, and high client-facing latency on Avere vFXT
rootCause: Cache policy set to ReadOnly while workload is write-heavy (>20% writes); write attempts cause permission errors or backpressure
solution: Change cache policy to ReadWrite or WriteBack to match the workload pattern; ReadOnly is designed for ~80% read / 20% write workloads

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet
symptom: Avere vFXT cache full with increased latency and 'recycling by space' visible on dashboard
rootCause: Cache storage capacity exceeded, triggering cache eviction (recycling by space) which competes with active I/O
solution: Monitor cache fullness via dashboard; add vFXT nodes to expand capacity, or adjust caching policy to reduce cache footprint

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet
symptom: Long-running revocations and stuck NFS operations on Avere vFXT node; conditions remain active on dashboard
rootCause: NFS operations stuck due to stale locks or revocations not clearing on a specific node
solution: Restart services on the node that first raised the condition to release stuck ops; use 'averecmd support.executeNormalMode node gsisupportbundle' to collect evidence first

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Avere vFXT Quick Reference Sheet
symptom: Swap pressure and performance degradation on Avere vFXT nodes with very long uptime
rootCause: Very long node uptime leads to accumulated swap usage and memory fragmentation drift
solution: Plan periodic node reboots as maintenance hygiene; use HA-mode reboot for minimal disruption or simultaneous reboot in maintenance window for faster completion

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Troubleshooting/RPC Slot Exhaustion - NFS
symptom: High client-facing latency on Avere vFXT with RPC slot exhaustion; req.hunt and req.asyncappend stats incrementing significantly, cwndlimit non-zero
rootCause: NFS RPC queue slots (default 256 per TCP connection) fully consumed due to delays between cluster nodes and core filer, causing operations to back up in the send queue
solution: Increase TCP connection multiplier (massX.nfsConnMult custom setting) from default 4 to 8 (max 23, typically ≤16); alternatively increase cluster IPs to create additional TCP connections. Verify core filer utilization <80% and no network congestion first.

sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - FXT and vFXT/Troubleshooting/RPC Slot Exhaustion - NFS
symptom: Avere vFXT high latency with recv.timerExpire counter incrementing (non-zero), often misdiagnosed as RPC slot exhaustion
rootCause: Core filer not responding to NFS operations within NfsBackEndTimeout (55s+); this is NOT RPC slot exhaustion but a core filer response failure
solution: Collect packet capture between cluster and core filer for analysis; investigate core filer health and network path; do NOT increase nfsConnMult as this is a different root cause from RPC slot exhaustion

symptom: Azure HPC Cache throughput cannot be changed after creation; customer needs more performance but cannot scale up or add nodes
rootCause: HPC Cache throughput/SKU is fixed at creation and cannot be scaled up or out by adding nodes, unlike Avere vFXT
solution: Destroy and recreate the HPC Cache with a larger throughput configuration/SKU; plan capacity requirements before initial deployment
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - HPC Cache/HPC Quick Reference Sheet

symptom: Azure HPC Cache queued requests, stuck operations, and latency spikes with all request slots consumed
rootCause: Slot exhaustion — all NFS request slots fully consumed, causing operations to queue and back up
solution: Align caching policy to workload, smooth client concurrency, cautiously increase slots only if backend and network can handle it; verify backend utilization <80%
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - HPC Cache/HPC Quick Reference Sheet

symptom: Azure HPC Cache elevated frontend latency caused by slow backend storage target
rootCause: Backend storage target (core filer) slowdowns propagate as elevated frontend (client-facing) latency; cache cannot mask backend delays beyond cached data
solution: Compare frontend vs storage target latency on Virtual Dashboard; investigate backend storage health, network path, and core filer utilization independently
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Avere - HPC Cache/HPC Quick Reference Sheet

symptom: Azure Container Storage (Arc) pod in CrashLoopBackOff — pod keeps restarting repeatedly
rootCause: Application failure or container misconfiguration causing the container process to exit with non-zero code on every restart
solution: 1) kubectl get pods -n <ns> to identify failing pod; 2) kubectl describe pod <name> -n <ns> for events; 3) kubectl logs <name> -n <ns> (--previous for last crash) to find root error
sourceRef: Supportability/AzureStorageDevices/AzureStorageDevices.wiki:/Azure Container Storage Enabled by Azure Arc/Common errors Kubernetes

symptom: Azure Container Storage (Arc) pods stuck in Pending state — cannot be scheduled
rootCause: Resource constraints (insufficient CPU/memory), unbound PersistentVolumeClaims, node taint not tolerated, or no schedulable nodes available
